## 2026-07-03T16:50:51.040Z - user

Topics: ux, autoplots


That’s a great name for the Codex sandbox.

I’d use Analytics Shiny App as the place to build the product shell around AutoPlots without risking AutoPlots itself.

The project mission should be:

Analytics Shiny App is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

It lets users:
- load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange plots into grids/tabs/sections
- preview outputs
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

The first AGENTS.md / Codex instruction file should be strict:

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)

Generated layout code must use:

AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

For the first Codex task, I’d keep it extremely bounded:

Create the initial Shiny app skeleton.

Requirements:
- app.R at project root
- app loads AutoPlots
- app has tabs: Data, Plots, Layout, Export
- Data tab can upload one CSV and preview it
- Plots tab lets user choose PlotType from Area, Line, Bar, Scatter, Histogram
- User can choose XVar, YVar, GroupVar from uploaded data columns
- User can enter title.text
- App renders one preview plot
- App shows generated AutoPlots R code
- Do not implement multi-plot layout yet
- Do not implement Electron packaging yet

Then second task:

Add multi-plot support.

Requirements:
- User can add current preview plot to a plot list
- Show list of created plots with names p1, p2, p3
- Layout tab previews AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Generated code includes plot creation code and display_plots_sections() code

That progression keeps it safe and gets you to the real product quickly.

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_types <- c("Area", "Line", "Bar", "Scatter", "Histogram")

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice())
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

build_autoplots_call <- function(plot_type, data, x_var, y_var, group_var, title_text) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", plot_type)
  args <- list(
    dt = data,
    XVar = selected_value(x_var),
    YVar = selected_value(y_var),
    GroupVar = selected_value(group_var),
    title.text = selected_value(title_text)
  )

  args <- args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
  do.call(plot_fun, args)
}

build_autoplots_code <- function(plot_type, x_var, y_var, group_var, title_text) {
  args <- c(
    "dt = data",
    paste0("XVar = ", r_string(selected_value(x_var))),
    paste0("YVar = ", r_string(selected_value(y_var))),
    if (!is.null(selected_value(group_var))) {
      paste0("GroupVar = ", r_string(selected_value(group_var)))
    },
    if (!is.null(selected_value(title_text))) {
      paste0("title.text = ", r_string(selected_value(title_text)))
    }
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    "p1 <- AutoPlots::", plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(args, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "p1"
  )
}

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Analytics Shiny App"),
  tabsetPanel(
    id = "main_tabs",
    tabPanel(
      "Data",
      sidebarLayout(
        sidebarPanel(
          fileInput("csv_file", "CSV", accept = c(".csv", "text/csv"))
        ),
        mainPanel(
          tableOutput("data_preview")
        )
      )
    ),
    tabPanel(
      "Plots",
      sidebarLayout(
        sidebarPanel(
          selectInput("plot_type", "PlotType", choices = plot_types),
          selectInput("x_var", "XVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          selectInput("y_var", "YVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          selectInput("group_var", "GroupVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          textInput("titl ... [truncated]

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

![Version:1.5.0](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=Version&message=1.5.0&color=blue&?style=plastic)


<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AdrianAntico/AutoPlots/master/inst/Logo2.PNG" align="center" width="800" />

<br> 

## ðŸš€ AutoPlots

AutoPlots is a comprehensive R visualization toolkit built on top of echarts4r, providing a consistent, ultra-customizable API across dozens of chart types â€” from classical analytics plots to advanced modeling visualizations and 3D charts.

AutoPlots exposes the full power of ECharts.js without requiring users to write JavaScript.
If a feature exists in ECharts, AutoPlots aims to make it available.

## â­ Why AutoPlots?

30+ high-quality plot types â€” all with consistent argument naming

Full access to ECharts options using the xxx.* naming convention

No JavaScript required

Shiny-ready out of the box

Supports multi-plot layouts via display_plots_grid()

3D visualizations, modeling plots, river charts, radar, parallel, contour, density, and more

Designed for data science, EDA, dashboards, and model evaluation


## ðŸ” Philosophy

AutoPlots is built around these principles:

Every plot is a standalone object
Modify, tweak, and filter each one independently.

Composition happens outside the plot
Use display_plots_grid() to arrange multiple plots into dashboards.

Expose full ECharts power without JS
Legend, toolbox, tooltip, axis, series, grid, animation, theme â€” everything is adjustable.

Beautiful defaults, infinite customization
Beginners get great plots immediately; experts can tune everything.

<br>

## Installation

```r
# Install CRAN dependencies
install.packages(c(
  "combinat", "data.table", "devtools", "dplyr", "e1071", "echarts4r",
  "lubridate", "nortest", "quanteda", "quanteda.textstats", "scales"
))

# Install AutoPlots from GitHub
devtools::install_github("AdrianAntico/AutoPlots", upgrade = FALSE, force = TRUE)
```

<br>

## Quick Start
```r
library(AutoPlots)
dt <- data.table::data.table(x = rnorm(1000))

AutoPlots::Density(dt, XVar = "x")

```

<br>

### Basic Plots
- Area
- Bar
- Box
- Copula
- Correlogram
- Density
- Donut
- Heatmaps
- Histogram
- Line
- Parallel
- Pie
- Radar
- River
- Rosetype
- Scatter
- Stacked Bar
- Step
- Treemap
- 3D Bar
- 3D Scatter
- 3D Copula
- Word Cloud

### Modeling Plots
- Autocorrelation
- Calibration
- Calibration Scatter
- Confusion Matrix Heatmap
- Gain
- Lift
- Partial Autocor ... [truncated]

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_types <- c("Area", "Line", "Bar", "Scatter", "Histogram")

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice())
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

build_autoplots_call <- function(plot_type, data, x_var, y_var, group_var, title_text) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", plot_type)
  args <- list(
    dt = data,
    XVar = selected_value(x_var),
    YVar = selected_value(y_var),
    GroupVar = selected_value(group_var),
    title.text = selected_value(title_text)
  )

  args <- args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
  do.call(plot_fun, args)
}

build_autoplots_code <- function(plot_type, x_var, y_var, group_var, title_text) {
  args <- c(
    "dt = data",
    paste0("XVar = ", r_string(selected_value(x_var))),
    paste0("YVar = ", r_string(selected_value(y_var))),
    if (!is.null(selected_value(group_var))) {
      paste0("GroupVar = ", r_string(selected_value(group_var)))
    },
    if (!is.null(selected_value(title_text))) {
      paste0("title.text = ", r_string(selected_value(title_text)))
    }
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    "p1 <- AutoPlots::", plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(args, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "p1"
  )
}

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Analytics Shiny App"),
  tabsetPanel(
    id = "main_tabs",
    tabPanel(
      "Data",
      sidebarLayout(
        sidebarPanel(
          fileInput("csv_file", "CSV", accept = c(".csv", "text/csv"))
        ),
        mainPanel(
          tableOutput("data_preview")
        )
      )
    ),
    tabPanel(
      "Plots",
      sidebarLayout(
        sidebarPanel(
          selectInput("plot_type", "PlotType", choices = plot_types),
          selectInput("x_var", "XVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          selectInput("y_var", "YVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          selectInput("group_var", "GroupVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          textInput("titl ... [truncated]

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_registry <- list(
  Area = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Line = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Bar = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Scatter = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Histogram = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Density = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Pie = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Donut = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  HeatMap = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar"), optional_mappings = character()),
  CorrMatrix = list(mappings = c("CorrVars"), optional_mappings = character())
)

plot_types <- names(plot_registry)

theme_choices <- c(
  "dark", "auritus", "azul", "bee-inspired", "blue", "caravan", "carp",
  "chalk", "cool", "dark-blue", "dark-bold", "dark-digerati",
  "dark-fresh-cut", "dark-mushroom", "eduardo", "essos", "forest",
  "fresh-cut", "fruit", "gray", "green", "halloween", "helianthus",
  "infographic", "inspired", "jazz", "london", "macarons", "macarons2",
  "mint", "purple-passion", "red", "red-velvet", "roma", "royal",
  "sakura", "v5", "walden", "wef", "weforum"
)

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE, label = "(none)") {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice(label))
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(label), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

selected_vector <- function(value) {
  value <- selected_value(value)
  if (is.null(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

logical_value <- function(value) {
  isTRUE(value)
}

numeric_or_null <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || is.na(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  as.numeric(value)
}

plot_spec <- function(plot_type) {
  plot_registry[[plot_type]]
}

mapping_input_id <- function(mapping) {
  paste0("mapping_", mapping)
}

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_registry <- list(
  Area = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Line = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Bar = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Scatter = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Histogram = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Density = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Pie = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Donut = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  HeatMap = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar"), optional_mappings = character()),
  CorrMatrix = list(mappings = c("CorrVars"), optional_mappings = character())
)

plot_types <- names(plot_registry)

theme_choices <- c(
  "dark", "auritus", "azul", "bee-inspired", "blue", "caravan", "carp",
  "chalk", "cool", "dark-blue", "dark-bold", "dark-digerati",
  "dark-fresh-cut", "dark-mushroom", "eduardo", "essos", "forest",
  "fresh-cut", "fruit", "gray", "green", "halloween", "helianthus",
  "infographic", "inspired", "jazz", "london", "macarons", "macarons2",
  "mint", "purple-passion", "red", "red-velvet", "roma", "royal",
  "sakura", "v5", "walden", "wef", "weforum"
)

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE, label = "(none)") {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice(label))
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(label), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

selected_vector <- function(value) {
  value <- selected_value(value)
  if (is.null(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

logical_value <- function(value) {
  isTRUE(value)
}

numeric_or_null <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || is.na(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  as.numeric(value)
}

plot_spec <- function(plot_type) {
  plot_registry[[plot_type]]
}

mapping_input_id <- function(mapping) {
  paste0("mapping_", mapping)
}

mapping_value <- function(input, m ... [truncated]

I read [AGENTS.md](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/AGENTS.md) and the AutoPlots README, then updated the app without modifying AutoPlots source.

What changed:
- Added a `plot_registry` for `Area`, `Line`, `Bar`, `Scatter`, `Histogram`, `Density`, `Pie`, `Donut`, `HeatMap`, and `CorrMatrix`.
- Mapping controls now change by `PlotType`.
- `HeatMap` uses `XVar`, `YVar`, `ZVar`.
- `CorrMatrix` uses multi-select `CorrVars`.
- Added common flat parameters from the README: `Theme`, `title.text`, `title.subtext`, `Height`, `Width`, `ShowLabels`, `MouseScroll`, `legend.show`, `xAxis.title`, `yAxis.title`, and `xAxis.axisLabel.rotate`.
- Generated code still uses `AutoPlots::<PlotType>()` with flat arguments.

Verification:
- `app.R` parses successfully with Rscript.
- `library(shiny); library(AutoPlots)` loads successfully.
- `source('app.R')` succeeds. Only warning was that `shiny` was built under R 4.2.3.

Update the Analytics Shiny App so plot-specific mappings and plot-specific UI options are driven by registries.

Context:
The current app has a basic plot_registry with mappings, but the UI still shows the same parameters for every plot type. Some parameters are not valid or useful for every AutoPlots plot. The app should use a registry-driven design so each plot type controls:
1. required mappings
2. optional mappings
3. available option controls

Important API doctrine:
- Do not redesign AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly.
- Generated code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Area(), etc.
- Keep flat AutoPlots parameter names exactly as-is, e.g. title.text, xAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate, visualMap.InRange.color.
- Do not introduce nested config objects.
- Do not add advanced customization beyond the registry controls in this task.
- Keep the app working.

Required changes:

1. Replace the existing plot_registry with a richer registry.

Output:
# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_registry <- list(
  Area = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Line = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Bar = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Scatter = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Histogram = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Density = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Pie = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Donut = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  HeatMap = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar"), optional_mappings = character()),
  CorrMatrix = list(mappings = c("CorrVars"), optional_mappings = character())
)

plot_types <- names(plot_registry)

theme_choices <- c(
  "dark", "auritus", "azul", "bee-inspired", "blue", "caravan", "carp",
  "chalk", "cool", "dark-blue", "dark-bold", "dark-digerati",
  "dark-fresh-cut", "dark-mushroom", "eduardo", "essos", "forest",
  "fresh-cut", "fruit", "gray", "green", "halloween", "helianthus",
  "infographic", "inspired", "jazz", "london", "macarons", "macarons2",
  "mint", "purple-passion", "red", "red-velvet", "roma", "royal",
  "sakura", "v5", "walden", "wef", "weforum"
)

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE, label = "(none)") {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice(label))
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(label), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

selected_vector <- function(value) {
  value <- selected_value(value)
  if (is.null(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

logical_value <- function(value) {
  isTRUE(value)
}

numeric_or_null <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || is.na(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  as.numeric(value)
}

plot_spec <- function(plot_type) {
  plot_registry[[plot_type]]
}

mapping_input_id <- function(mapping) {
  paste0("mapping_", mapping)
}

Updated [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R) with the registry-driven structure you outlined.

What changed:
- `plot_registry` now defines each plot’s required mappings, optional mappings, and active options.
- Added `option_registry` as the source of truth for rendering flat AutoPlots option controls.
- Added `option_control()` and `option_value()`.
- Replaced hardcoded sidebar option controls with `uiOutput("option_inputs")`.
- `build_plot_args()` now only passes mappings and options declared for the selected plot type.
- Generated code uses the same registry path, so hidden/inactive options are omitted automatically.

- `app.R` parses.
- `source("app.R")` succeeds.
- Smoke checks passed for the tricky cases:

  - Pie excludes `GroupVar`
  - Histogram excludes `YVar`

Output:
Fix two issues in the Analytics Shiny App:

1. File uploads currently allow data of any size. Add an explicit upload size limit.
2. When PlotType changes, XVar/YVar/ZVar/CorrVars reset because mapping inputs are rebuilt. Preserve mapping selections when possible.

Rules:
- Do not redesign the app.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep the registry-driven UI design.
- Make small, targeted changes only.

Part A: Add upload size limit

Add a constant near the top of app.R:

MAX_UPLOAD_MB <- 50

Then add:

options(shiny.maxRequestSize = MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2)

Also enforce file size manually inside uploaded_data() using input$csv_file$size.

Current uploaded_data() likely does:

uploaded_data <- reactive({
  req(input$csv_file)
  data.table::fread(input$csv_file$datapath)
})

Replace it with logic like:

uploaded_data <- reactive({
  req(input$csv_file)

  if (!is.null(input$csv_file$size) &&
      input$csv_file$size > MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Uploaded file is too large. Limit is %s MB.",
        MAX_UPLOAD_MB
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  data.table::fread(input$csv_file$datapath)
})

Then update output$data_preview to catch and display the error cleanly instead of crashing the app.

For example:

output$data_preview <- renderTable({
  tryCatch(
    head(uploaded_data(), 25),
    error = function(e) {
      data.frame(Message = conditionMessage(e))
    }
  )
})

Also make preview_plot() fail gracefully when uploaded_data() errors.

Part B: Preserve mapping inputs across PlotType changes

Problem:
mapping_inputs is rebuilt dynamically. When PlotType changes, selectInput controls are destroyed and recreated, so XVar and YVar reset.

Add a reactiveValues object in server:

mapping_state <- reactiveValues(
  values = list()
)

Add observers to remember current mapping selections whenever they change.

Mappings to track:
- XVar
- YVar
- ZVar
- GroupVar
- CorrVars

Use mapping_input_id(mapping) to get the input id.

Implement a helper inside server or globally:

remember_mapping <- function(mapping) {
  observeEvent(input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]], {
    mapping_state$values[[mapping]] <- input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]]
  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)
}

Then call it for:

lapply(c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar", "GroupVar", "CorrVars"), remember_mapping)

Update mapping_control() so it can take ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-03T18:07:53.133Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Implemented the next patch in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R), preserving your trimmed registry choices.

What changed:
- Added `MAX_UPLOAD_MB <- 50` and `options(shiny.maxRequestSize = MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2)`.
- Added manual upload-size validation before `data.table::fread()` runs.
- Data preview now shows a friendly error row instead of crashing.
- Preview plot now fails gracefully on upload/data errors.
- Added `data_summary` above the preview table with filename, MB, rows, and columns.
- Added `mapping_state` so `XVar`, `YVar`, `ZVar`, `GroupVar`, and `CorrVars` are remembered across PlotType changes when still valid.
- Updated `mapping_control()` to accept and validate preserved selections.

Verified:
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- Oversized upload path returns: `Uploaded file is too large. Limit is 50 MB.`
- Small CSV summary works.
- `Line -> Area -> Histogram -> Line` preserves X/Y state as expected.
- `CorrVars` persists when switching away and back.
- Generated code filtering still omits inactive mappings/options.


## 2026-07-03T18:15:39.640Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Next up: Fix the Analytics Shiny App so plots are not automatically built when entering the Plots tab or when changing PlotType/mappings.

Problem:
The app currently tries to build the plot automatically whenever inputs change. This causes failures when:
- the user first enters the Plots tab
- PlotType changes and mappings are temporarily incomplete
- aggregation is enabled but not fully configured
- active_data() is not yet plot-ready
- required mapping inputs are blank or invalid

Goal:
Only build or refresh the preview plot when the user clicks a button.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep the registry-driven plot/option UI.
- Make small, targeted changes.

Required changes:

1. Add a button to the Plots sidebar.

In the Plots sidebar, after the mapping and option controls, add:

actionButton("build_plot", "Build / Refresh Plot", class = "btn-primary")

Optional but preferred:
Add a small help text:
"Plot preview updates only when this button is clicked."

2. Add validation helpers.

Create a helper function:

validate_plot_ready <- function(plot_type, data, input) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return("No data is available.")
  }

  spec <- plot_spec(plot_type)

  for (mapping in spec$mappings) {
    value <- mapping_value(input, mapping)

    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || any(value == "")) {
      return(paste0("Required mapping is missing: ", mapping))
    }

    if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))
      if (length(missing_cols)) {
        return(paste0("CorrVars contains columns not in data: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
      }
    } else {
      if (!value %in% names(data)) {
        return(paste0(mapping, " column is not in data: ", value))
      }
    }
  }

  TRUE
}

If aggregation is implemented, also validate aggregation before active_data() tries to aggregate:
- If use_aggregation is TRUE and agg_method != "count", require agg_metric_var.
- If group vars or metric var are not in uploaded_data(), return a friendly message.
Do not throw raw errors into the UI.

3. Replace preview_plot <- reactive(...) with eventReactive.

Current code probably has:

preview_plot <- reactive({
  req(uploaded_data(), input$plot_type)
  ...
  build_autoplots_call(...)
})

Replace it with:

preview_plot <- eventReactive(input$build_plot, {
  data <- tryCatch(active_data(), error = function(e) {
    stop(conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
  })

  ready <- validate_plot_ready(input$plot_type, data, input)

  if (!isTRUE(ready)) {
    stop(ready, call. = FALSE)
  }

  build_autoplots_call(
    plot_type = input$plot_type,
    data = data,
    input = input
  )
}, ignoreInit = TRUE)

If active_data() does not exist yet, use uploaded_data() for now.

4. Update output$preview_plot to handle no plot yet and friendly errors.

Replace any simple renderUI with something like:

output$preview_plot <- renderUI({
  if (input$build_plot == 0L) {
    return(tags$div(
      style = "padding: 16px; color: #6B7280;",
      "Configure the plot options, then click Build / Refresh Plot."
    ))
  }

  tryCatch(
    {
      htmltools::tagList(preview_plot())
    },
    error = function(e) {
      tags$div(
        style = paste0(
          "padding: 14px;",
          "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
          "border-radius: 8px;",
          "background: #FEF2F2;",
          "color: #991B1B;"
        ),
        tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
        tags$br(),
        conditionMessage(e)
      )
    }
  )
})

5. Generated code can remain reactive.

The generated code panel may continue to update as users change inputs. That is okay. But it should not require the plot to be successfully built.

6. When PlotType changes, clear the previous preview or show a stale-preview warning.

Preferred simple approach:
Add an observer:

observeEvent(input$plot_type, {
  # Do not build automatically.
  # Optionally update a text output saying preview may be stale.
})

Do not force plot rebuild.

Alternative:
Use a reactiveVal for last_built_plot_type and show:
"Plot type changed. Click Build / Refresh Plot to update preview."

This is optional for now.

7. Smoke tests:
- Enter Plots tab after uploading data. It should not attempt to build automatically.
- Change PlotType several times. It should not error.
- Select incomplete mappings. It should not error until Build / Refresh Plot is clicked.
- Click Build / Refresh Plot with incomplete mappings. It should show a friendly validation message.
- Click Build / Refresh Plot with valid mappings. It should render the plot.
- Change PlotType after rendering. The app should not crash.
- Generated code should still update normally.

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  mapping_state <- reactiveValues(values = list())

  remember_mapping <- function(mapping) {
    force(mapping)
    observeEvent(input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]], {
      mapping_state$values[[mapping]] <- input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]]
    }, ignoreInit = TRUE)
  }

  lapply(c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar", "GroupVar", "CorrVars"), remember_mapping)

  uploaded_data <- reactive({
    req(input$csv_file)

    if (!is.null(input$csv_file$size) &&
        input$csv_file$size > MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2) {
      stop(
        sprintf(
          "Uploaded file is too large. Limit is %s MB.",
          MAX_UPLOAD_MB
        ),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }

    data.table::fread(input$csv_file$datapath)
  })

  output$mapping_inputs <- renderUI({
    req(input$plot_type)

    data <- tryCatch(uploaded_data(), error = function(e) {
      stop(conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
    })

    ready <- validate_plot_ready(input$plot_type, data, input)
    if (!isTRUE(ready)) {
      stop(ready, call. = FALSE)
    }

    build_autoplots_call(
      plot_type = input$plot_type,
      data = data,
      input = input
    )
  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)

  output$preview_plot <- renderUI({
    if (input$build_plot == 0L) {
      return(tags$div(
        style = "padding: 16px; color: #6B7280;",
        "Configure the plot options, then click Build / Refresh Plot."
      ))
    }

    tryCatch(
      {
        htmltools::tagList(preview_plot())
      },
      error = function(e) {
        tags$div(
          style = paste0(
            "padding: 14px;",
            "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
            "border-radius: 8px;",
            "background: #FEF2F2;",
            "color: #991B1B;"
          ),
          tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
          tags$br(),
          conditionMessage(e)
        )
      }
    )
  })

  output$generated_code <- renderText({
    req(input$plot_type)

    build_autoplots_code(
      plot_type = input$plot_type,
      input = input
    )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

## 2026-07-03T18:21:57.207Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Updated [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R) so plots only build when the user clicks `Build / Refresh Plot`.

What changed:
- Added the `Build / Refresh Plot` button and help text in the Plots sidebar.
- Added `validate_plot_ready()` for required mappings and missing-column checks.
- Replaced automatic `preview_plot <- reactive(...)` with `eventReactive(input$build_plot, ...)`.
- Added a neutral placeholder before the first build.
- Added friendly plot error rendering for validation, upload-size, and AutoPlots build errors.
- Kept generated code reactive, so it still updates as inputs change without forcing a plot build.

Verified:
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- Entering Plots / changing PlotType does not build automatically.
- Placeholder appears before the first build.
- Generated code updates for PlotType changes and still excludes inactive mappings like `YVar` for Histogram.
- Oversized upload plus build click shows the friendly size-limit message.
- `validate_plot_ready()` catches missing and invalid required mappings.


## 2026-07-03T18:44:58.859Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Fix the double-click-to-render bug in the Analytics Shiny App.

Problem:
After adding Build / Refresh Plot, clicking the button once often shows the red "Plot could not be built" error, but clicking it a second time builds successfully. This suggests the build event is firing while dynamically generated mapping inputs are stale, NULL, or not fully initialized.

Goal:
One click should build the plot when visible inputs are valid.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven UI.
- Make small targeted changes.

Required approach:
Use a plot configuration snapshot at button click time.

1. Add a helper to snapshot current plot config.

Create:

snapshot_plot_config <- function(plot_type, input) {
  spec <- plot_spec(plot_type)

  mappings <- list()
  for (mapping in active_mappings(plot_type)) {
    mappings[[mapping]] <- mapping_value(input, mapping)
  }

  options <- list()
  for (option_name in spec$options) {
    options[[option_name]] <- option_value(input, option_name)
  }

  list(
    plot_type = plot_type,
    mappings = mappings,
    options = options
  )
}

2. Add build args from snapshot.

Create:

build_plot_args_from_config <- function(config, data, include_data = TRUE) {
  args <- list()

  if (include_data) {
    args$dt <- data
  }

  for (name in names(config$mappings)) {
    args[[name]] <- config$mappings[[name]]
  }

  for (name in names(config$options)) {
    args[[name]] <- config$options[[name]]
  }

  args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
}

3. Add plot call from snapshot.

Create:

build_autoplots_call_from_config <- function(config, data) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", config$plot_type)
  args <- build_plot_args_from_config(config, data = data, include_data = TRUE)
  do.call(plot_fun, args)
}

4. Update validation to validate the snapshot, not live input.

Create:

validate_plot_config_ready <- function(config, data) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return("No data is available.")
  }

  spec <- plot_spec(config$plot_type)

  for (mapping in spec$mappings) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]

    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || any(value == "")) {
      return(paste0("Required mapping is missing: ", mapping))
    }

    if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))
      if (length(missing_cols)) {
        return(paste0(
          "CorrVars contains columns not in data: ",
          paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")
        ))
      }
    } else {
      if (!value %in% names(data)) {
        return(paste0(mapping, " column is not in data: ", value))
      }
    }
  }

  TRUE
}

5. Update preview_plot eventReactive.

Replace the current preview_plot eventReactive with logic like:

preview_plot <- eventReactive(input$build_plot, {
  config <- isolate(snapshot_plot_config(input$plot_type, input))

  data <- tryCatch(
    isolate(active_data()),
    error = function(e) {
      stop(conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
    }
  )

  ready <- validate_plot_config_ready(config, data)

  if (!isTRUE(ready)) {
    stop(ready, call. = FALSE)
  }

  build_autoplots_call_from_config(
    config = config,
    data = data
  )
}, ignoreInit = TRUE)

If active_data() does not exist yet, use uploaded_data() inside isolate().

6. Do not use live input values inside the plot build after this point.

The plot build should use:
- config$plot_type
- config$mappings
- config$options
- data snapshot

It should not reach back into input during do.call.

7. Make the red error message less aggressive for the first empty state.

If input$build_plot == 0, keep showing:
"Configure the plot options, then click Build / Refresh Plot."

Only show the red error after the user actually clicks the button.

8. Add a small debounce/settle protection if needed.

If the double-click issue persists, wrap the button in a tiny delay using session$onFlushed.

Preferred simple approach:
Do not add delay unless the snapshot fix fails.

9. Smoke tests:
- Upload data.
- Go to Plots tab.
- Choose Line, XVar, YVar.
- Click Build / Refresh Plot once. It should render.
- Change PlotType to Area. Click once. It should render.
- Change PlotType to Histogram. Click once. It should render using XVar only.
- Change PlotType to Line. Click once. It should render with previous YVar if still valid.
- Enable aggregation if implemented. Configure aggregation. Click once. It should render.

  - config-based validation
  - config-based AutoPlots call

- `app.R` parses and sources.

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  mapping_state <- reactiveValues(values = list())

  remember_mapping <- function(mapping) {
    force(mapping)
    observeEvent(input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]], {
      mapping_state$values[[mapping]] <- input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]]
    }, ignoreInit = TRUE)
  }

  lapply(c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar", "GroupVar", "CorrVars"), remember_mapping)

  uploaded_data <- reactive({
    req(input$csv_file)

    if (!is.null(input$csv_file$size) &&
        input$csv_file$size > MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2) {
      stop(
        sprintf(
          "Uploaded file is too large. Limit is %s MB.",
          MAX_UPLOAD_MB
        ),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }

    data.table::fread(input$csv_file$datapath)
  })

  output$mapping_inputs <- renderUI({
    req(input$plot_type)
    data <- tryCatch(uploaded_data(), error = function(e) NULL)
    spec <- plot_spec(input$plot_type)

    tagList(
      lapply(spec$mappings, function(mapping) {
        mapping_control(
          mapping = mapping,
          data = data,
          required = TRUE,
          selected = mapping_state$values[[mapping]]
        )
      }),
      lapply(spec$optional_mappings, function(mapping) {
        mapping_control(
          mapping = mapping,
          data = data,
          r ... [truncated]

  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)

  output$preview_plot <- renderUI({

      return(tags$div(
        style = "padding: 16px; color: #6B7280;",
        "Configure the plot options, then click Build / Refresh Plot."
      ))
    }

    if (is.null(input$csv_file)) {
      return(plot_error_message("No data is available."))
    }

    if (!is.null(input$csv_file$size) &&
        input$csv_file$size > MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2) {
      return(plot_error_message(sprintf(
        "Uploaded file is too large. Limit is %s MB.",
        MAX_UPLOAD_MB
      )))
    }

    tryCatch(
      {
        plot <- preview_plot()
        if (inherits(plot, "plot_error")) {
          return(plot_error_message(as.character(unclass(plot))))
        }

        htmltools::tagList(plot)
      },
      error = function(e) {
        plot_error_message(conditionMessage(e))
      }
    )
  })

  output$generated_code <- renderText({
    req(input$plot_type)

    build_autoplots_code(
      plot_type = input$plot_type,
      input = input
    )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)


## 2026-07-03T18:48:20.134Z - assistant

I found the remaining weak spot: the build still snapshots directly from `input`, so if Shiny has just recreated a selectInput and the browser has not round-tripped its value yet, the snapshot can still see `NULL`. I’m adding two protections together: wait until the next flush after the button click, and fall back to the remembered mapping state while taking the snapshot.


## 2026-07-03T18:48:31.109Z - assistant

Now I’m wiring the build event through a post-flush `build_request`, so the render path won’t try to consume the plot result until Shiny has settled the dynamic controls.


## 2026-07-03T18:48:51.640Z - assistant

The flush queue and fallback snapshot are patched. I’m running parse/source plus a focused test that simulates the stale-input case: live `YVar` is blank, remembered `YVar` is valid, and the snapshot should still build a valid config.

## 2026-07-03T18:49:20.568Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


I made a second fix for the double-click issue in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Added a `build_request <- reactiveVal(0L)` queue.
- The build button now schedules the build with `session$onFlushed(..., once = TRUE)`, so Shiny gets one UI flush to finish creating/updating dynamic mapping inputs before the snapshot is taken.
- `preview_plot` now listens to `build_request()` instead of directly to `input$build_plot`.
- Added a neutral `"Preparing plot preview..."` state while the post-flush build is pending.
- Updated `snapshot_plot_config()` to fall back to remembered `mapping_state` values when a just-rebuilt input is temporarily `NULL` or blank.

Verified:
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- Snapshot fallback uses remembered mappings correctly.
- Post-flush build request advances after `session$flushReact()` in a Shiny server smoke test.

This should address the “first click sees stale/NULL inputs, second click works” pattern directly.

Yes, I think you’re onto the real issue: AutoPlots returns an `echarts4r`/`htmlwidget` object. Returning that widget through `renderUI()` is the shaky part. ## 2026-07-03T18:52:36.485Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Yes, I think that was the issue. AutoPlots returns an `echarts4r` / `htmlwidget` object, and we were trying to display it through `renderUI()` with `htmltools::tagList(plot)`. That is shaky for Shiny widgets and matches the blank “Plot could not be built” behavior you’re seeing.

I updated [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R) so:

- Plot creation still uses only `AutoPlots::<PlotType>()`.
- The preview UI now creates `echarts4r::echarts4rOutput("preview_plot_widget")`.
- The plot itself renders through `echarts4r::renderEcharts4r({ preview_plot() })`.

That uses echarts4r only as the Shiny rendering mechanism, not for chart construction or configuration, so it stays within the AutoPlots wrapper doctrine.

Verified:
- `AutoPlots::Area(...)` returns `echarts4r` / `htmlwidget`.
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- Shiny smoke test confirms the preview now emits the `preview_plot_widget` echarts output container after one build click.

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  mapping_state <- reactiveValues(values = list())
  build_request <- reactiveVal(0L)

  remember_mapping <- function(mapping) {
    force(mapping)
    observeEvent(input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]], {
      mapping_state$values[[mapping]] <- input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]]
    }, ignoreInit = TRUE)
  }

  lapply(c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar", "GroupVar", "CorrVars"), remember_mapping)

  observeEvent(input$build_plot, {
    requested_build <- input$build_plot

    session$onFlushed(function() {
      build_request(requested_build)
    }, once = TRUE)
  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)

  uploaded_data <- reactive({
    req(input$csv_file)

    if (!is.null(input$csv_file$size) &&
        input$csv_file$size > MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2) {
      stop(
        sprintf(
          "Uploaded file is too large. Limit is %s MB.",
          MAX_UPLOAD_MB
        ),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }

    data.table::fread(input$csv_file$datapath)
  })

  output$mapping_inputs <- renderUI({
    req(input$plot_type)
    data <- tryCatch(uploaded_data(), error = function(e) NULL)
    spec <- plot_spec(input$plot_type)

    tagList(
      lapply(spec$mappings, function(mapping) {
        mapping_control(
          mapping = mapping,
          data = data,
          required = TRUE,
          selected = mapping_state$values[[mapping]]
        )
      }),
      lapply(spec$optional_mappings, function(mapping) {
        mapping_control(
          mapping = mapping,
          data = data,
          required = FALSE,
          selected = mapping_state$values[[mapping]]
        )
      })
    )
  })

  output$option_inputs <- renderUI({
    req(input$plot_type)
    spec <- plot_spec(input$plot_type)

    tagList(lapply(spec$options, option_control))
  })

  output$data_summary <- renderText({
    req(input$csv_file)

    tryCatch({
      data <- uploaded_data()
      sprintf(
        "%s - %s MB - %s rows x %s columns",
        input$csv_file$name,
        file_size_mb(input$csv_file$size),
        format(nrow(data), big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE),
        format(ncol(data), big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE)
      )
    }, ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-03T18:55:28.301Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


You were right: the app should use the boring, proven pattern here. I replaced the over-engineered `eventReactive`/flush/snapshot build flow with a direct button observer.

In [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R), the build path is now:

- `observeEvent(input$build_plot, { ... })`
- grab current `input` values
- grab uploaded data
- validate mappings
- call `AutoPlots::<PlotType>()`
- store the resulting widget in `plot_result <- reactiveVal(NULL)`
- render with `echarts4r::renderEcharts4r({ plot_result() })`

I kept `echarts4r` only for rendering the AutoPlots htmlwidget, not for building charts.

Also changed the error handling so blank errors become visible as:
`AutoPlots returned an error without a message.`

Verified:
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- A direct `AutoPlots::Area(...)` call returns `echarts4r` / `htmlwidget`.
- Shiny smoke test confirms one button click stores an `echarts4r` plot and emits the preview widget output container.

## 2026-07-03T18:58:09.665Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


You’re right, the error message is the key: `Some items of .SDcols are not column names: []`. That strongly points to us passing an empty string as a column argument, almost certainly optional `GroupVar = ""`, even though the generated code hides it. I’m going to fix the argument filtering so optional blank mappings never reach AutoPlots.

I noticed the first helper version was too broad for non-character values like the data table itself. I’m tightening it so it only treats character blanks as empty, while leaving `FALSE`, numeric values, and `dt` alone.

## 2026-07-03T18:58:48.551Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Read it. The error was not a renderer issue:

`Some items of .SDcols are not column names: []`

That means AutoPlots/data.table was receiving an empty column name. In this case, `GroupVar = ""` was still getting passed from the optional `(none)` select, even though generated code did not show it.

I fixed [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R) so blank optional arguments are removed before calling AutoPlots:

- Added `is_empty_arg()`
- Updated `snapshot_plot_config()` so it does not fall back to remembered blank mappings
- Updated `build_plot_args_from_config()` so `GroupVar = ""` is omitted from the actual AutoPlots call

Verified with the exact case:
- `Area`
- `XVar = "date"`
- `YVar = "y"`
- `GroupVar = ""`

The resulting args no longer include `GroupVar`, and `AutoPlots::Area(...)` builds an `echarts4r` widget successfully.

#' @param EchartsLabels character
#' @param TimeLine logical
#' @param MouseScroll logical, zoom via mouse scroll
#' @param AggMethod character
#' @param GroupVar Character variable
#' @param Debug Debugging purposes
#' @return plot
#' @export
PartialDependence.Box <- function(dt = NULL,
                                  PreAgg = TRUE,
                                  SampleSize = 100000L,
                                  XVar = NULL,
                                  YVar = NULL,
                                  ZVar = NULL,
                                  GroupVar = NULL,
                                  YVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  XVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  ZVarTrans = "Identity",
                                  FacetRows = 1,
                                  FacetCols = 1,
                                  FacetLevels = NULL,
                                  NumberBins = 20,
                                  AggMethod = "mean",
                                  Height = NULL,
                                  Width = NULL,
                                  ShowLabels = FALSE,
                                  Theme = "dark",
                                  MouseScroll = FALSE,
                                  EchartsLabels = FALSE,
                                  TimeLine = FALSE,
                                  title.text = NULL,
                                  yAxis.title = NULL,
                                  xAxis.title = NULL,
                                  Debug = FALSE) {

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })

  GroupVar <- NULL

  # Minimize data before moving on
  if(Debug) print("Plot.PartialDependence.Box # Minimize data before moving on")
  Ncols <- ncol(dt)
  if(Ncols > 3L) {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt[, .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, ZVar)])
  } else {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt)
  }

  # If actual is in factor form, convert to numeric
  if(Debug) print("Plot.PartialDependence.Box # If actual is in factor form, convert to numeric")
  if(!is.numeric(dt1[[YVar]])) {
    data.table::set(dt1, j = YVar, value = as.numeric(as.character(dt1[[YVar]])))
  }

  # Add a column that ranks predicted values
  dt1[, eval(XVar) := as.character(round(data.table::frank(get(XVar)) * (NumberBins) / .N) / NumberBins)]
  dt1[, ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-03T19:27:26.623Z - assistant

Topics: autonls, autoquant_shap, autoplots


Inspected only [R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R). No files modified.

**Exported Functions**

`Density`, `ProbabilityPlot`, `Histogram`, `Treemap`, `Pie`, `Donut`, `Rosetype`, `Box`, `WordCloud`, `Line`, `Area`, `Step`, `River`, `Bar`, `ACF`, `PACF`, `StackedBar`, `BarPlot3D`, `HeatMap`, `Radar`, `CorrMatrix`, `Parallel`, `Copula`, `Copula3D`, `Scatter`, `Scatter3D`, `Residuals.Histogram`, `Residuals.Scatter`, `Calibration.Line`, `Calibration.Box`, `PartialDependence.Line`, `PartialDependence.Box`, `PartialDependence.HeatMap`, `VariableImportance`, `ROC`, `ConfusionMatrix`, `Lift`, `Gains`, `BinaryMetricsPlot`, `ShapImportance`.

**PreAgg / AggMethod**

Have both `PreAgg` and `AggMethod`:
`Treemap`, `Pie`, `Donut`, `Rosetype`, `Line`, `Area`, `Step`, `River`, `Bar`, `StackedBar`, `BarPlot3D`, `HeatMap`, `Radar`, `PartialDependence.Box`, `ConfusionMatrix`, `BinaryMetricsPlot`, `ShapImportance`.

Have `PreAgg` only:
`CorrMatrix`, `Lift`, `Gains`.

Have `AggMethod` only:
`ACF`, `PACF`, `Residuals.Histogram`, `Residuals.Scatter`, `Calibration.Line`, `Calibration.Box`, `PartialDependence.Line`, `PartialDependence.HeatMap`, `VariableImportance`, `ROC`.

Have neither:
`Density`, `ProbabilityPlot`, `Histogram`, `Box`, `WordCloud`, `Parallel`, `Copula`, `Copula3D`, `Scatter`, `Scatter3D`.

**Prep / Aggregation Inventory**

| Function(s) | Prep / aggregation location |
|---|---|
| `Density`, `ProbabilityPlot`, `Box`, `Scatter`, `Scatter3D`, `Copula`, `Copula3D` | Coerce/copy/subset data, then render; no explicit `PreAgg` aggregation. |
| `Histogram` | Coerces/subsets, then internally bins and counts records by bucket/group. |
| `WordCloud` | Coerces data, uses `quanteda.textstats::textstat_frequency()`, then renames/selects frequency columns. |
| `Treemap` | Uses `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)` and aggregates inside `if (!PreAgg)` around lines 3327 and 3611-3624; also performs hierarchy/rate prep before plotting. |
| `Pie`, `Donut`, `Rosetype` | Shared pattern: `if (!PreAgg)`, coerce, resolve `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)`, then aggregate numeric vars by grouping vars. |
| `Line`, `Area`, `Step` | Shared pattern: coerce, optional melt for multi-`YVar`, subset columns, then `if (!PreAgg)` aggregate by `XVar` and optional `GroupVar`; sort afterward. |
| `River` | Similar to line-family prep: subset `YVar`, `XVar`, `GroupVar`, optional melt, then `if (!PreAgg)` aggregate by `XVar`/`GroupVar`. |
| `Bar`, `StackedBar` | Shared bar aggregation style: coerce, `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)`, optional faceting subset, then repeated `if (!PreAgg)` branches aggregate `.SD` by grouping vars. |
| `BarPlot3D`, `HeatMap` | Shared 3D/grid prep: subset `XVar`, `YVar`, `ZVar`, optional rank/bin/top-N prep, then aggregate `ZVar` by `XVar`/`YVar` when not pre-aggregated. |
| `Radar` | Coerces/subsets `YVar` and `GroupVar`; uses `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)` inside `if (!PreAgg)`. |
| `CorrMatrix`, `Parallel` | Both select `CorrVars` and use `stats::na.omit()`; `CorrMatrix` has `PreAgg` but no `AggMethod`, while `Parallel` has neither. |
| `ACF`, `PACF` | Coerce/copy, resolve `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)`, aggregate `YVar` by `DateVar`, then compute ACF/PACF data. |
| `Residuals.Histogram`, `Residuals.Scatter` | Coerce and subset `XVar`, `YVar`, `GroupVar`; residual histogram removes `YVar` for plotting residual distribution, scatter ranks/bins `XVar`. |
| `Calibration.Line`, `Calibration.Box` | Coerce/copy, rank/bin predicted values into percentiles; line version aggregates with `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)`, box version prepares `Target - Predicted`. |
| `PartialDependence.Line`, `PartialDependence.Box`, `PartialDependence.HeatMap` | Coerce/copy, rank/bin predictors, compute `Target - Predicted`; line/heatmap use `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)`, box delegates prepared data to `AutoPlots::Box()`. |
| `VariableImportance` | Coerces data; has `AggMethod` parameter but minimal visible prep in this function body. |
| `ROC`, `Lift`, `Gains` | Shared model-eval prep: subset/melt multiclass or grouped data, create grouped labels, transpose/rbind/derive curve data; `ROC` has `AggMethod`, `Lift`/`Gains` have `PreAgg`. |
| `ConfusionMatrix` | Coerce/copy/subset, count by `XVar`/`YVar`, with alternate classification path when `!PreAgg`. |
| `BinaryMetricsPlot` | Coerce/copy/subset, melt prepared metric data, then delegates plotting. |
| `ShapImportance` | In `if (!PreAgg)`, coerce/subset, resolve `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)`, aggregate SHAP variables by `GroupVar` or overall, then melt. |

**Similar Prep Logic**

Most similar groups are:

`Line` / `Area` / `Step` / `River`: time/sequence style `XVar` + `YVar` + optional `GroupVar`, optional melt, optional aggregation, sort.

`Pie` / `Donut` / `Rosetype`: category/value aggregation with nearly identical `PreAgg` + `AggMethod` flow.

`Bar` / `StackedBar`: categorical bar aggregation with similar `.SD` grouping branches.

`BarPlot3D` / `HeatMap`: `XVar`/`YVar`/`ZVar` grid prep, ranking/binning, and matrix-like aggregation.

`CorrMatrix` / `Parallel`: `CorrVars` selection plus NA omission.

`Calibration.*` / `PartialDependence.*` / `ROC` / `Lift` / `Gains` / `BinaryMetricsPlot`: model-evaluation prep, often deriving transformed metrics before delegating to another AutoPlots function.


## 2026-07-03T19:29:35.762Z - user

Create a new refactor candidate file for the Line / Area / Step / River prep logic.

Target file:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW_REFAC_PREP.R

Do not modify R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
Do not modify any existing exported functions.
Do not touch Bar, StackedBar, Pie, Donut, Rosetype, HeatMap, BarPlot3D, Treemap, Radar, or model-evaluation functions.

Goal:
Create a candidate shared data-prep helper for the sequence/time-series plotting family and candidate refactored versions of Line, Area, Step, and River so we can QA behavior before merging.

Use only the current APIs and function names from R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
One naming note

Since the real new functions are Line, Area, etc., the candidate names should be:

Line.Refac()
Area.Refac()
Step.Refac()
River.Refac() 

Important semantics:
- PreAgg = TRUE means the input data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
- PreAgg = FALSE means the function should aggregate internally.
- Preserve existing behavior as closely as possible.
- Use data.table.
- Do not introduce nested config objects.
- Do not change public parameter names.

1. Create internal helper:

.ap_prep_sequence_plot_data <- function(
  dt,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  DualYVar = NULL,
  GroupVar = NULL,
  PreAgg = TRUE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  FacetLevels = NULL,
  YVarTrans = "Identity",
  DualYVarTrans = "Identity",
  XVarTrans = "Identity",
  plot_family = c("line", "area", "step", "river"),
  Debug = FALSE
)

The helper should preserve the existing prep behavior shared by Line, Area, Step, and River:

- ensure dt is a data.table or convert it
- copy input data before modifying
- if GroupVar exists and XVar is missing, set XVar <- GroupVar and GroupVar <- NULL, matching current Line/Area/Step behavior
- convert factor GroupVar to character
- for Line/Area/Step:
  - error if length(YVar) > 1 and DualYVar is supplied
  - error if GroupVar and DualYVar are both supplied
  - if length(YVar) > 1, melt data long
  - if multiple YVars plus GroupVar, preserve the current combined group label behavior
  - subset to required columns
  - if GroupVar exists and FacetLevels supplied, filter to FacetLevels
  - if !PreAgg, aggregate using SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  - aggregate by XVar or XVar + GroupVar
  - sort by XVar or GroupVar + XVar
  - apply YVarTrans to YVar
  - apply DualYVarTrans to DualYVar
  - convert IDate XVar to Date
  - convert IDateTime XVar to POSIXct
- for River:
  - preserve existing River-specific behavior
  - return NULL or an error-compatible object when existing River logic would return NULL
  - handle one YVar + GroupVar behavior as current River does
  - preserve aggregation by XVar / XVar + GroupVar when !PreAgg
  - preserve multi-YVar behavior
  - sort and date-convert XVar as current River does

Return a list:

list(
  dt = dt1,
  XVar = XVar,
  YVar = YVar,
  DualYVar = DualYVar,
  GroupVar = GroupVar
)

2. Add candidate functions with new names only:

Line.Refac
Area.Refac
Step.Refac
River.Refac

Each candidate function should:
- have the same function signature as the existing Line, Area, Step, or River function in PlotFunctions_NEW.R
- call .ap_prep_sequence_plot_data()
- use the returned dt/XVar/YVar/DualYVar/GroupVar values
- preserve existing rendering code as closely as possible
- not be exported in NAMESPACE yet

3. Add QA helper:

  if (is.null(GroupVar) & length(YVar) == 1 & is.null(DualYVar)) legend.show <- FALSE
  if(TimeLine && length(FacetLevels) == 0L) X_Scroll <- FALSE

  # Correct args
  if(length(GroupVar) > 0L && length(XVar) == 0L) {
    XVar <- GroupVar
    GroupVar <- NULL
  }

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)

  # Theme overrides
  apply_theme_defaults(Theme, plot_type = "River", grouped = !is.null(GroupVar), env = environment())
  if (is.null(GroupVar) & length(YVar) > 1) legend.show <- FALSE

  if(length(GroupVar) == 0L) TimeLine <- FALSE
  if(length(GroupVar) == 0L && length(YVar) <= 1L) {
    if(Debug) print("if(length(GroupVar) == 0L && length(YVar) <= 1L) return(NULL)")
    return(NULL)
  }
  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })
  Ncols <- ncol(dt)
  if(length(FacetLevels) > 1L) {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt[get(GroupVar) %in% c(eval(FacetLevels)), .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, GroupVar)])
  } else {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt[, .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, GroupVar)])
  }

  if(Debug) print("Plot.River 3")

  # Minimize data before moving on
  if(!PreAgg) {

    if(Debug) print("Plot.River 4")

    # DCast -> redefine YVar -> Proceed as normal
    if(length(YVar) > 1L && length(GroupVar) == 0L) {
      dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(
        data = dt1,
        id.vars = XVar,
        measure.vars = YVar,
        variable.name = "Group",
        value.name = "Measures")
      YVar <- "Measures"
      GroupVar <- "Group"
    } else {
      dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt)
    }

    # Define Aggregation function
    if(Debug) print("Plot.Calibration.Line # Define Aggregation function")
    aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)

    # Aggregate data
    if(length(GroupVar) > 0L) {
      dt1 <- dt1[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), by = c(XVar,GroupVar[1L])]
      data.table::setorderv(x = dt1, cols = c(GroupVar[1L], XVar), rep(1L, length(c(GroupVar[1L], XVar))))
    } else {
      dt1 <- dt1[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), by = c(XVar)]
      data.table::setorderv(x = dt1, cols = XVar, 1L)
    }
  }

  # Transformation
  for(yvart in YVarTrans) {
    if(YVarTrans != "Identity") {
      dt1 <- AutoTransformationCreate(data = dt1, ColumnNames = yvart, Methods = YVarTrans)$Data
    }
  }

  if(Debug) print("Plot.River 6b")

  # Plot
  data.table::setorderv(x = dt1, cols = XVar, 1L)
  cxv <- class(dt1[[XVar]])[1L]
  if(cxv %in% "IDate") {
    dt1[, eval(XVar) := as.Date(get(XVar))]
  } else if(cxv %in% "IDateTime") {
    dt1[, eval(XVar) := as.POSIXct(get(XVar))]
  }

  if(Debug) print("Plot.River 7b")

  # Build base plot depending on GroupVar availability

  dt,

    XVar <- GroupVar

      data.table::setDT(dt),
      error = function(x) dt <<- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
    )

    dt_work[, eval(GroupVar[1L]) := as.character(get(GroupVar[1L]))]
  }

expression(.ap_prep_sequence_plot_data <- function(dt, XVar, 
    YVar, DualYVar = NULL, GroupVar = NULL, PreAgg = TRUE, AggMethod = "mean", 
    FacetLevels = NULL, YVarTrans = "Identity", DualYVarTrans = "Identity", 
    XVarTrans = "Identity", plot_family = c("line", "area", "step", 
        "river"), Debug = FALSE) {
    plot_family <- match.arg(plot_family)
    if (length(GroupVar) > 0L && length(XVar) == 0L && plot_family %in% 
        c("line", "area", "step")) {
        XVar <- GroupVar
        GroupVar <- NULL
    }
    if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
        tryCatch(data.table::setDT(dt), error = function(x) dt <<- data.table::as.data.table(dt))
    }
    dt_work <- data.table::copy(dt)
    if (length(GroupVar) > 0L && class(dt_work[[GroupVar[1L]]])[1L] == 
        "factor") {
        dt_work[, `:=`(eval(GroupVar[1L]), as.character(get(GroupVar[1L])))]
    }
    if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
        if (length(YVar) > 1L && length(DualYVar) > 0L) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
        }
        if (length(GroupVar) > 0L && length(DualYVar) > 0L) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
        }
        if (length(YVar) > 1L && length(DualYVar) == 0L) {
            if (length(GroupVar) > 0L) {
                dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(data = dt_work, 
                  id.vars = c(XVar, GroupVar), measure.vars = YVar, 
                  variable.name = "Measures", value.name = "Values")
                dt1[, `:=`(GroupVars, paste0(Measures, get(GroupVar[1L])))]
                dt1[, `:=`(Measures, NULL)]
                dt1[, `:=`(eval(GroupVar[1L]), NULL)]
                GroupVar <- "GroupVars"
                YVar <- "Values"
            }
            else {
                dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(data = dt_work, 
                  id.vars = XVar, measure.vars = YVar, variable.name = "Measures", 
                  value.name = "Values")
                GroupVar <- "Measures"
                YVar <- "Values"
            }
        }
        else {
            dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt_work)
        }
        if (ncol(dt1) > 2L && length(GroupVar) == 0L) {
            dt1 <- dt1[, .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, DualYVar)]
        }

.ap_qa_sequence_refactor_examples <- function() {
  data <- data.table::data.table(
    DateTime = as.Date(c("2026-01-01", "2026-01-01", "2026-01-02", "2026-01-02", "2026-01-03", "2026-01-03")),
    Segment = c("A", "B", "A", "B", "A", "B"),
    Revenue = c(10, 20, 12, 22, 14, 24),
    Cost = c(4, 8, 5, 9, 6, 10)

    old_line_no_group = Line(dt = data, XVar = "DateTime", YVar = "Revenue", PreAgg = FALSE),
    new_line_no_group = Line.Refac(dt = data, XVar = "DateTime", YVar = "Revenue", PreAgg = FALSE),
    old_line_group = Line(dt = data, XVar = "DateTime", YVar = "Revenue", GroupVar = "Segment", PreAgg = FALSE),

    new_area_group = Area.Refac(dt = data, XVar = "DateTime", YVar = "Revenue", GroupVar = "Segment", PreAgg = FALSE),

    new_step_group = Step.Refac(dt = data, XVar = "DateTime", YVar = "Revenue", GroupVar = "Segment", PreAgg = FALSE),

Revise the Line / Area / Step / River refactor candidate so the prep helper is cleaner and easier to reason through, not just mechanically extracted.

Target file:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW_REFAC_PREP.R

Do not modify R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R yet.

Goal:
Improve the readability and maintainability of .ap_prep_sequence_plot_data() while preserving behavior from Line, Area, Step, and River.

Rules:
- Do not change public APIs.
- Do not export the candidate functions yet.
- Do not touch original Line, Area, Step, River in PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not touch Bar, StackedBar, Pie, Donut, HeatMap, BarPlot3D, or model-evaluation functions.
- Keep PreAgg semantics unchanged:
  PreAgg = TRUE means data is already plot-ready.
  PreAgg = FALSE means aggregate internally.
- Use data.table.
- Make behavior explicit and readable.
- Avoid clever metaprogramming.
- Avoid broad style rewrites outside this candidate file.

Refactor direction:

1. Break .ap_prep_sequence_plot_data() into small internal helpers.

Add helpers such as:

.ap_not_empty <- function(x)
.ap_first_or_null <- function(x)
.ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x)
.ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt)
.ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars)
.ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family)
.ap_melt_sequence_yvars <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar)
.ap_subset_sequence_columns <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, FacetLevels)
.ap_aggregate_sequence_data <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, AggMethod)
.ap_apply_sequence_transforms <- function(dt, YVar, DualYVar, YVarTrans, DualYVarTrans)
.ap_sort_and_convert_sequence_x <- function(dt, XVar, GroupVar)

Do not add all helpers if unnecessary, but split enough that the main prep function reads like a simple pipeline.

2. Make the main helper read like this conceptually:

.ap_prep_sequence_plot_data <- function(...) {
  plot_family <- match.arg(plot_family)

  dt1 <- .ap_as_dt_copy(dt)

  vars <- .ap_normalize_sequence_vars(...)

  .ap_validate_sequence_inputs(...)

  dt1 <- .ap_factor_to_character(dt1, vars that need it)

  if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {

  }

  if (!PreAgg) {

  }

  dt1 <- .ap_apply_sequence_transforms(...)

# This is a refactor candidate only.
# It does not replace PlotFunctions_NEW.R yet.
# It is intended for QA before merging.

.ap_prep_sequence_plot_data <- function(
  dt,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  DualYVar = NULL,
  GroupVar = NULL,
  PreAgg = TRUE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  FacetLevels = NULL,
  YVarTrans = "Identity",
  DualYVarTrans = "Identity",
  XVarTrans = "Identity",
  plot_family = c("line", "area", "step", "river"),
  Debug = FALSE
) {
  plot_family <- match.arg(plot_family)

  if (length(GroupVar) > 0L && length(XVar) == 0L && plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
    XVar <- GroupVar
    GroupVar <- NULL
  }

  if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
    tryCatch(
      data.table::setDT(dt),
      error = function(x) dt <<- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
    )
  }

  dt_work <- data.table::copy(dt)

  if (length(GroupVar) > 0L && class(dt_work[[GroupVar[1L]]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt_work[, eval(GroupVar[1L]) := as.character(get(GroupVar[1L]))]
  }

  if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
    if (length(YVar) > 1L && length(DualYVar) > 0L) {
      stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
    }
    if (length(GroupVar) > 0L && length(DualYVar) > 0L) {
      stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
    }

    if (length(YVar) > 1L && length(DualYVar) == 0L) {
      if (length(GroupVar) > 0L) {
        dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(
          data = dt_work,
          id.vars = c(XVar, GroupVar),
          measure.vars = YVar,
          variable.name = "Measures",
          value.name = "Values"
        )
        dt1[, GroupVars := paste0(Measures, GroupVar)]
        dt1[, Measures := NULL]
        dt1[, eval(GroupVar[1L]) := NULL]
        GroupVar <- "GroupVars"
        YVar <- "Values"
      } else {
        dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(
          data = dt_work,
          id.vars = XVar,
          measure.vars = YVar,
          variable.name = "Measures",
          value.name = "Values"
        )
        GroupVar <- "Measures"
        YVar <- "Values"
      }
    } else {
      dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt_work)
    }

    if (ncol(dt1) > 2L && length(GroupVar) == 0L) {
      dt1 <- dt1[, .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, DualYVar)]
    } else if (length(GroupVar) > 0L) {
      dt1 <- dt1[, .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, DualYVar, GroupVar[1L])]
      if (length(FacetLevels) > 0L) {
        dt1 <- dt1[get(GroupVar[1L]) ... [truncated]

expression(.ap_not_empty <- function(x) {
    !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0L && !all(is.na(x)) && !all(x == 
        "")
}, .ap_first_or_null <- function(x) {
    if (.ap_not_empty(x)) 
        x[1L]
    else NULL
}, .ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x) {
    if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) 
        return(NULL)
    x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
    if (length(x) == 0L) 
        NULL
    else x
}, .ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt) {
    if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
        dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
    }
    data.table::copy(dt)
}, .ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars) {
    vars <- .ap_clean_var_vector(vars)
    for (var in vars) {
        if (var %in% names(dt) && class(dt[[var]])[1L] == "factor") {
            dt[, `:=`(eval(var), as.character(get(var)))]
        }
    }
    dt
}, .ap_normalize_sequence_vars <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, 
    GroupVar, plot_family) {
    XVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(XVar)
    YVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(YVar)
    DualYVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(DualYVar)
    GroupVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(GroupVar))
    if (.ap_not_empty(GroupVar) && !.ap_not_empty(XVar) && plot_family %in% 
        c("line", "area", "step")) {
        XVar <- GroupVar
        GroupVar <- NULL
    }
    list(XVar = XVar, YVar = YVar, DualYVar = DualYVar, GroupVar = GroupVar)
}, .ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, 
    GroupVar, plot_family) {
    has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
    has_dual_y <- .ap_not_empty(DualYVar)
    has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
    if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
        if (has_multiple_y && has_dual_y) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
        }
        if (has_group && has_dual_y) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
        }
    }
    invisible(TRUE)
}, .ap_melt_sequence_yvars <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar) {
    has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
    has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
    if (!has_multiple_y) {
        return(list(dt = data.table::copy(dt), YVar = YVar, GroupVar = GroupVar))
    }
    if (!has_group) {
        dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(data = dt, id.vars = XVar,

            value.name = "Values")
        return(list(dt = dt1, YVar = "Values", GroupVar = "Measures"))
    } ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-03T19:42:52.358Z - assistant

Revised [R/PlotFunctions_NEW_REFAC_PREP.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW_REFAC_PREP.R) only.

What changed:
- Split `.ap_prep_sequence_plot_data()` into small helpers for var cleanup, data copying, validation, melting, River shaping, subsetting, aggregation, transforms, and sorting/date conversion.
- Kept `Line.Refac`, `Area.Refac`, `Step.Refac`, and `River.Refac` unexported and thin.
- Added `.ap_qa_sequence_refactor_data()` for inspecting prepared data directly.
- Added `.ap_compare_dt_shape()` for simple old/new data shape checks.
- Preserved current odd-but-important behaviors like multi-Y melting, combined group labels, DualY restrictions, River’s special `NULL` case, and `PreAgg = TRUE` skipping aggregation.

Verification passed:
- Parsed `R/PlotFunctions_NEW_REFAC_PREP.R`
- Sourced `AccessoryFunctions.R`, `PlotFunctions_NEW.R`, and the refactor file
- Ran `.ap_qa_sequence_refactor_data()`
- Sourced render helpers and ran `.ap_qa_sequence_refactor_examples()`
- All old/new plots returned `echarts4r/htmlwidget` classes.

- Do not update River yet.
- Do not modify Bar, StackedBar, Pie, Donut, Rosetype, HeatMap, BarPlot3D, Treemap, Radar, or model-evaluation functions.
- Do not change public function signatures.
- Do not export the internal helpers.
- Preserve PreAgg semantics:
  PreAgg = TRUE means data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
  PreAgg = FALSE means aggregate internally.
- Preserve existing rendering logic as much as possible.
- Keep Line, Area, and Step output behavior unchanged.
- Do not add smart rounding or unrelated features.

Implementation:
1. Add the helper functions near the top of PlotFunctions_NEW.R, before the plotting functions that use them.
2. Replace the duplicated prep block in Line with a call to .ap_prep_sequence_plot_data(plot_family = "line").
3. Replace the duplicated prep block in Area with a call to .ap_prep_sequence_plot_data(plot_family = "area").
4. Replace the duplicated prep block in Step with a call to .ap_prep_sequence_plot_data(plot_family = "step").
5. Unpack returned values:
   prep <- .ap_prep_sequence_plot_data(...)
   dt1 <- prep$dt
   XVar <- prep$XVar
   YVar <- prep$YVar
   DualYVar <- prep$DualYVar
   GroupVar <- prep$GroupVar
6. Leave rendering code alone except for using the prepared variables.
7. Do not copy Line.Refac, Area.Refac, Step.Refac, or River.Refac into the production file.
8. After changes, source R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R and verify:
   - Line renders with PreAgg = TRUE
   - Line renders with PreAgg = FALSE
   - Line renders with GroupVar
   - Line renders with multiple YVars
   - Area renders with GroupVar
   - Step renders with GroupVar
   - DualYVar still works when GroupVar is NULL
   - DualYVar + GroupVar still errors
   - multiple YVars + DualYVar still errors
9. Show the diff.
