# This is a refactor candidate only.
# It does not replace PlotFunctions_NEW.R yet.
# It is intended for QA before merging.

.ap_not_empty <- function(x) {
  !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0L && !all(is.na(x)) && !all(x == "")
}

.ap_first_or_null <- function(x) {
  if (.ap_not_empty(x)) x[1L] else NULL
}

.ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x) {
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) return(NULL)
  x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
  if (length(x) == 0L) NULL else x
}

.ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt) {
  if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  }
  data.table::copy(dt)
}

.ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars) {
  vars <- .ap_clean_var_vector(vars)
  for (var in vars) {
    if (var %in% names(dt) && class(dt[[var]])[1L] == "factor") {
      dt[, eval(var) := as.character(get(var))]
    }
  }
  dt
}

.ap_normalize_sequence_vars <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family) {
  XVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(XVar)
  YVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(YVar)
  DualYVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(DualYVar)
  GroupVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(GroupVar))

  if (.ap_not_empty(GroupVar) && !.ap_not_empty(XVar) && plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
    XVar <- GroupVar
    GroupVar <- NULL
  }

  list(
    XVar = XVar,
    YVar = YVar,
    DualYVar = DualYVar,
    GroupVar = GroupVar
  )
}

.ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family) {
  has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
  has_dual_y <- .ap_not_empty(DualYVar)
  has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L

  if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
    # Dual-axis rendering is only implemented for a single ungrouped Y series.
    if (has_multiple_y && has_dual_y) {
      stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
    }
    if (has_group && has_dual_y) {
      stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
    }
  }

  invisible(TRUE)
}

.ap_melt_sequence_yvars <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar) {
  has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
  has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L

  if (!has_multiple_y) {
    return(list(dt = data.table::copy(dt), YVar = YVar, GroupVar = GroupVar))
  }

  # Multiple Y columns are represented as long data so the render path can
  # treat each measure as a series without changing public plotting args.
  if (!has_group) {
    dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(
      data = dt, ... [truncated]

+.ap_not_empty <- function(x) {
+  !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0L && !all(is.na(x)) && !all(x == "")
+}
+
+.ap_first_or_null <- function(x) {
+  if (.ap_not_empty(x)) x[1L] else NULL
+}
+
+.ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x) {
+  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) return(NULL)
+  x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
+  if (length(x) == 0L) NULL else x
+}
+
+.ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt) {
+  if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
+    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
+  }
+  data.table::copy(dt)
+}
+
+.ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars) {
+  vars <- .ap_clean_var_vector(vars)
+  for (var in vars) {
+    if (var %in% names(dt) && class(dt[[var]])[1L] == "factor") {
+      dt[, eval(var) := as.character(get(var))]
+    }
+  }
+  dt
+}
+
+.ap_normalize_sequence_vars <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family) {
+  XVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(XVar)
+  YVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(YVar)
+  DualYVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(DualYVar)
+  GroupVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(GroupVar))
+
+  if (.ap_not_empty(GroupVar) && !.ap_not_empty(XVar) && plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
+    XVar <- GroupVar
+    GroupVar <- NULL
+  }
+
+  list(
+    XVar = XVar,
+    YVar = YVar,
+    DualYVar = DualYVar,
+    GroupVar = GroupVar
+  )
+}
+
+.ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family) {

+    if (has_group && has_dual_y) {

+    }
+  }
+
+  invisible(TRUE)
+}

+    return(list(dt = data.table::copy(dt), YVar = ... [truncated]

.ap_prepare_river_shape <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar, FacetLevels, PreAgg, Debug = FALSE) {
  has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
  has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L

  # River currently has no useful one-series/no-group shape and returns NULL.
  if (!has_group && !has_multiple_y) {
    if (Debug) print("if(length(GroupVar) == 0L && length(YVar) <= 1L) return(NULL)")
    return(NULL)
  }

  if (has_group && length(FacetLevels) > 1L) {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt[get(GroupVar) %in% c(eval(FacetLevels)), .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, GroupVar)])
  } else {
    dt1 <- data.table::copy(dt[, .SD, .SDcols = c(YVar, XVar, GroupVar)])
  }

  # Existing River only melts multiple Y columns into a synthetic group before
  # internal aggregation; pre-aggregated input keeps the current wide shape.
  if (!PreAgg && has_multiple_y && !has_group) {
    dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(
      data = dt1,
      id.vars = XVar,
      measure.vars = YVar,
      variable.name = "Group",
      value.name = "Measures"
    )
    return(list(dt = dt1, YVar = "Measures", GroupVar = "Group"))
  }

  list(dt = dt1, YVar = YVar, GroupVar = GroupVar)
}

.ap_subset_sequence_columns <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, FacetLevels) {
  has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
  cols <- c(YVar, XVar, DualYVar, GroupVar)

  if (ncol(dt) <= 2L && !has_group) {
    return(dt)
  }

  dt1 <- dt[, .SD, .SDcols = cols]

  if (has_group && length(FacetLevels) > 0L) {
    dt1 <- dt1[get(GroupVar) %in% eval(FacetLevels)]
  }

  dt1
}

.ap_aggregate_sequence_data <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, AggMethod) {
  has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
  by_vars <- XVar
  if (has_group) {
    by_vars <- c(XVar, GroupVar)
  }

  measure_vars <- c(YVar, DualYVar)
  aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)

  dt1 <- dt[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFu ... [truncated]

expression(.ap_not_empty <- function(x) {
    !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0L && !all(is.na(x)) && !all(x == 
        "")
}, .ap_first_or_null <- function(x) {
    if (.ap_not_empty(x)) 
        x[1L]
    else NULL
}, .ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x) {
    if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) 
        return(NULL)
    x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
    if (length(x) == 0L) 
        NULL
    else x
}, .ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt) {
    if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
        dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
    }
    data.table::copy(dt)
}, .ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars) {
    vars <- .ap_clean_var_vector(vars)
    for (var in vars) {
        if (var %in% names(dt) && class(dt[[var]])[1L] == "factor") {
            dt[, `:=`(eval(var), as.character(get(var)))]
        }
    }
    dt
}, .ap_normalize_sequence_vars <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, 
    GroupVar, plot_family) {
    XVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(XVar)
    YVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(YVar)
    DualYVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(DualYVar)
    GroupVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(GroupVar))
    if (.ap_not_empty(GroupVar) && !.ap_not_empty(XVar) && plot_family %in% 
        c("line", "area", "step")) {
        XVar <- GroupVar
        GroupVar <- NULL
    }
    list(XVar = XVar, YVar = YVar, DualYVar = DualYVar, GroupVar = GroupVar)
}, .ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, 
    GroupVar, plot_family) {
    has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
    has_dual_y <- .ap_not_empty(DualYVar)
    has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
    if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
        if (has_multiple_y && has_dual_y) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
        }
        if (has_group && has_dual_y) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
        }
    }
    invisible(TRUE)
}, .ap_melt_sequence_yvars <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar) {
    has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
    has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
    if (!has_multiple_y) {
        return(list(dt = data.table::copy(dt), YVar = YVar, GroupVar = GroupVar))
    }
    if (!has_group) {
        dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(data = dt, id.vars = XVar, 
            measure.vars = YVar, variable.name = "Measures", 
            value.name = "Values")
        return(list(dt = dt1, YVar = "Values", Grou ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-03T20:19:13.571Z - assistant

Topics: autoquant_shap, autoplots


You were right. River needed to reshape multiple `YVar`s even when `PreAgg = TRUE`.

I updated [R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R) so the River branch in `.ap_prepare_river_shape()` now melts no-group multi-Y data into:

- `Group`
- `Measures`

regardless of `PreAgg`. Then `PreAgg = TRUE` skips aggregation, while `PreAgg = FALSE` aggregates the melted data as before.

Verified:
- `River(..., YVar = c("Revenue", "Cost"), PreAgg = FALSE)` renders
- `River(..., YVar = c("Revenue", "Cost"), PreAgg = TRUE)` renders
- `River(..., YVar = "Revenue", GroupVar = "Segment", PreAgg = FALSE)` renders
- `River(..., YVar = "Revenue", GroupVar = "Segment", PreAgg = TRUE)` renders
- `parse(file='R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R')` passes


## 2026-07-03T20:21:43.536Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Slick. Next up: Continue the AutoPlots aggregation/prep refactor while context is fresh.

Target file:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R

Reference file if useful:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW_REFAC_PREP.R

Goal:
Refactor and clean the shared prep/aggregation logic for Pie, Donut, and Rosetype.

Rules:
- Modify only R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not modify the Shiny app.
- Do not touch Line, Area, Step, or River; those already passed QA.
- Do not touch Bar, StackedBar, HeatMap, BarPlot3D, Treemap, Radar, or model-evaluation functions in this task.
- Do not change public function signatures.
- Do not export internal helpers.
- Preserve PreAgg semantics:
  PreAgg = TRUE means input data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
  PreAgg = FALSE means AutoPlots should aggregate internally.
- Preserve existing visual behavior as much as possible.
- Use data.table.
- Avoid dplyr in prep helpers.
- Make the prep logic easier to reason through, not just mechanically extracted.

Current pattern to preserve:
Pie, Donut, and Rosetype share nearly identical prep:
- normalize XVar and YVar to first value
- require both XVar and YVar
- if !PreAgg:
  - coerce data to data.table
  - resolve SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  - infer numeric measure variable(s)
  - infer grouping variable(s)
  - aggregate numeric measure vars by grouping vars
  - convert numeric grouping vars to character for category plotting
- if PreAgg:
  - copy dt
  - identify numeric and character vars
- apply YVarTrans to numeric vars
- build the same echarts pie-series rendering currently used

Implementation:
1. Create an internal helper:

.ap_prep_pie_family_data <- function(
  dt,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  GroupVar = NULL,
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  YVarTrans = "Identity",
  XVarTrans = "Identity",
  Debug = FALSE
)

Return:

list(
  dt = temp,
  XVar = XVar,
  YVar = YVar,
  GroupVar = GroupVar,
  numvars = numvars,
  byvars = byvars
)

2. Break helper into smaller helpers if useful.

Allowed internal helpers:
- .ap_prep_pie_identify_roles()
- .ap_aggregate_pie_family_data()
- .ap_convert_grouping_vars_to_character()

Reuse any existing internal helpers already added for sequence prep when appropriate, such as:
- .ap_not_empty
- .ap_first_or_null
- .ap_clean_var_vector
- .ap_as_dt_copy
- .ap_factor_to_character

Do not duplicate helper definitions if they already exist in PlotFunctions_NEW.R.

3. Replace the prep blocks inside Pie, Donut, and Rosetype with calls to .ap_prep_pie_family_data().

4. Keep rendering logic separate.

Pie should still render normal pie.
Donut should still use radius = c("50%", "70%").
Rosetype should still use roseType = "radius".

5. Add comments for tricky behavior:
- PreAgg = TRUE skips internal aggregation
- role inference exists because either XVar or YVar may be numeric/category-like
- numeric grouping vars are converted to character for categorical display

6. Add a temporary internal QA helper:

.ap_qa_pie_family_refactor_examples <- function()

It should create fake data and return old/new or at least current rendered plots for:
- Pie with PreAgg = FALSE, AggMethod = "sum"
- Donut with PreAgg = FALSE, AggMethod = "mean"
- Rosetype with PreAgg = FALSE, AggMethod = "median"
- Pie with PreAgg = TRUE using already aggregated data
- Factor XVar case
- Numeric XVar / categorical YVar role-inference case if supported by current behavior

Since old functions are being replaced in-place, if old/new comparison is hard, return rendered plots and prepared data outputs from .ap_prep_pie_family_data().

7. After changes, source R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R and verify:
- Pie renders with PreAgg = FALSE
- Donut renders with PreAgg = FALSE
- Rosetype renders with PreAgg = FALSE
- Pie renders with PreAgg = TRUE and pre-aggregated data
- Existing Line / Area / Step / River examples still source/render

8. Show the diff.

                Debug = FALSE) {

  apply_theme_defaults(Theme, plot_type = "Pie", grouped = FALSE, env = environment())
  if(length(YVar) > 0L) YVar <- YVar[1L]
  if(length(XVar) > 0L) XVar <- XVar[1L]

  # Used multiple times
  check1 <- length(XVar) != 0 && length(YVar) != 0

  if(!PreAgg) {
    if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
      dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
    })
    aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  }

  # Convert factor to character
  if(length(GroupVar) > 0L && class(dt[[GroupVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(GroupVar) := as.character(get(GroupVar))]
  }

  if(length(XVar) > 0L && class(dt[[XVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(XVar) := as.character(get(XVar))]
  }

  # Create base plot object

  byvars <- c()
  if(check1) {
    if(!PreAgg) {
      if(tryCatch({class(dt[[eval(YVar)]])[1L]}, error = function(x) "bla") %in% c('numeric','integer')) {
        numvars <- unique(c(numvars, YVar))
      } else {
        byvars <- unique(c(byvars, YVar))
      }
      if(tryCatch({class(dt[[eval(XVar)]])[1L]}, error = function(x) "bla") %in% c('numeric','integer')) {
        if(length(numvars) > 0) {
          x <- length(unique(dt[[XVar]]))
          y <- length(unique(dt[[YVar]]))
          if(x > y) {
            byvars <- unique(c(byvars, YVar))
            numvars[1L] <- XVar
          } else {
            byvars <- unique(c(byvars, XVar))
          }

          numvars <- unique(c(numvars, XVar))
        }
      } else {
        byvars <- unique(c(byvars, XVar))

      if(!is.null(byvars)) {
        temp <- dt[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = c(numvars), keyby = c(byvars)]

          if(class(temp[[i]])[1L] %in% c('numeric','integer')) {
            temp[, eval(i) := as.character(get(i))]
          }
        }
      } else {
        temp <- dt[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = c(numvars)]
      }
    } else {
      temp <- data.table::copy(dt)
      numvars <- ColNameFilter(data = temp, Types = 'numeric')[[1L]]
      byvars <- unlist(ColNameFilter(data = temp, Types = "character"))
    }

    # Transformation
    if(YVarTrans != "Identity") {
      temp <- AutoTransformationCreate(data = temp, ColumnNames = numvars, Methods = YVarTrans)$Data

#' @family Standard Plots

#' @param dt source data.table

#' @param YVar Y-Axis variable name

#' @param FacetRows Defaults to 1 which causes no faceting to occur vertically. Otherwise, supply a numeric value for the number of output grid rows
#' @param FacetCols Defaults to 1 which causes no faceting to occur horizontally. Otherwise, supply a numeric value for the number of output grid columns
#' @param FacetLevels Faceting rows x columns is the max number of levels allowed in a grid. If your GroupVar has more you can supply the levels to display.

#' @param ShowLabels character

expression(.ap_not_empty <- function(x) {
    !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0L && !all(is.na(x)) && !all(x == 
        "")
}, .ap_first_or_null <- function(x) {
    if (.ap_not_empty(x)) 
        x[1L]
    else NULL
}, .ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x) {
    if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) 
        return(NULL)
    x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
    if (length(x) == 0L) 
        NULL
    else x
}, .ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt) {
    if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
        dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
    }
    data.table::copy(dt)
}, .ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars) {
    vars <- .ap_clean_var_vector(vars)
    for (var in vars) {
        if (var %in% names(dt) && class(dt[[var]])[1L] == "factor") {
            dt[, `:=`(eval(var), as.character(get(var)))]
        }
    }
    dt
}, .ap_normalize_sequence_vars <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, 
    GroupVar, plot_family) {
    XVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(XVar)
    YVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(YVar)
    DualYVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(DualYVar)
    GroupVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(GroupVar))
    if (.ap_not_empty(GroupVar) && !.ap_not_empty(XVar) && plot_family %in% 
        c("line", "area", "step")) {
        XVar <- GroupVar
        GroupVar <- NULL
    }
    list(XVar = XVar, YVar = YVar, DualYVar = DualYVar, GroupVar = GroupVar)
}, .ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, 
    GroupVar, plot_family) {
    has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
    has_dual_y <- .ap_not_empty(DualYVar)
    has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
    if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
        if (has_multiple_y && has_dual_y) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
        }
        if (has_group && has_dual_y) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
        }
    }
    invisible(TRUE)
}, .ap_melt_sequence_yvars <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar) {
    has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
    has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
    if (!has_multiple_y) {
        return(list(dt = data.table::copy(dt), YVar = YVar, GroupVar = GroupVar))
    }
    if (!has_group) {
        dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(data = dt, id.vars = XVar, 
            measure.vars = YVar, variable.name = "Measures",

        return(list(dt = dt1, YVar = "Values", Grou ... [truncated]

diff --git a/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R b/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R
index 2b78123..fec0690 100644
--- a/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R
+++ b/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R
@@ -14,6 +14,420 @@
 # You should have received a copy of the GNU Affero General Public License
 # along with this program. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 
+.ap_not_empty <- function(x) {
+  !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0L && !all(is.na(x)) && !all(x == "")
+}
+
+.ap_first_or_null <- function(x) {
+  if (.ap_not_empty(x)) x[1L] else NULL
+}
+
+.ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x) {
+  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) return(NULL)
+  x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
+  if (length(x) == 0L) NULL else x
+}
+
+.ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt) {
+  if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
+    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
+  }
+  data.table::copy(dt)
+}
+
+.ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars) {
+  vars <- .ap_clean_var_vector(vars)
+  for (var in vars) {
+    if (var %in% names(dt) && class(dt[[var]])[1L] == "factor") {
+      dt[, eval(var) := as.character(get(var))]
+    }
+  }
+  dt
+}
+
+.ap_normalize_sequence_vars <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family) {
+  XVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(XVar)
+  YVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(YVar)
+  DualYVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(DualYVar)
+  GroupVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(GroupVar))
+
+  if (.ap_not_empty(GroupVar) && !.ap_not_empty(XVar) && plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
+    XVar <- GroupVar
+    GroupVar <- NULL
+  }
+
+  list(
+    XVar = XVar,
+    YVar = YVar,
+    DualYVar = DualYVar,
+    GroupVar = GroupVar
+  )
+}
+
+.ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family) {
+  has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
+  has_dual_y <- .ap_not_empty(DualYVar)
+  has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
+
+  if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
+    # Dual-axis rendering is only implemented for a single ungrouped Y series.
+    if (has_multiple_y && has_dual_y) {
+      stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
+    }
+    if (has_group && has_dual_y) {
+      stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
+    }
+  }
+
+  invisible(TRUE)
+}
+
+.ap_melt_sequence_yvars <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar) {

diff --git a/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R b/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R
index 2b78123..452509b 100644
--- a/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R
+++ b/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R
@@ -15,4 +15,418 @@
 # along with this program. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 
+.ap_not_empty <- function(x) {
+  !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0L && !all(is.na(x)) && !all(x == "")
+}
+
+.ap_first_or_null <- function(x) {
+  if (.ap_not_empty(x)) x[1L] else NULL
+}
+
+.ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x) {
+  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) return(NULL)
+  x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
+  if (length(x) == 0L) NULL else x
+}
+
+.ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt) {
+  if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
+    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
+  }
+  data.table::copy(dt)
+}
+
+.ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars) {
+  vars <- .ap_clean_var_vector(vars)
+  for (var in vars) {
+    if (var %in% names(dt) && class(dt[[var]])[1L] == "factor") {
+      dt[, eval(var) := as.character(get(var))]
+    }
+  }
+  dt
+}
+
+.ap_normalize_sequence_vars <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family) {
+  XVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(XVar)
+  YVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(YVar)
+  DualYVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(DualYVar)
+  GroupVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(GroupVar))
+
+  if (.ap_not_empty(GroupVar) && !.ap_not_empty(XVar) && plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
+    XVar <- GroupVar
+    GroupVar <- NULL
+  }
+
+  list(
+    XVar = XVar,
+    YVar = YVar,
+    DualYVar = DualYVar,
+    GroupVar = GroupVar
+  )
+}
+
+.ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, GroupVar, plot_family) {
+  has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
+  has_dual_y <- .ap_not_empty(DualYVar)
+  has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
+
+  if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
+    # Dual-axis rendering is only implemented for a single ungrouped Y series.
+    if (has_multiple_y && has_dual_y) {
+      stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
+    }
+    if (has_group && has_dual_y) {
+      stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
+    }
+  }
+
+  invisible(TRUE)
+}
+
+.ap_melt_sequence_yvars <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar) {
+  has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
+  has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
+
+  if (!has_multiple_y) {

## 2026-07-03T21:00:43.899Z - assistant

One thing jumped out: `e_legend_full()` defaults `legend.height` to `240px`, but Rosetype’s public default overrides that with `NULL`. Still, the object has legend data. I’m going to patch Rosetype to treat slice legends like Pie/Donut explicitly and keep `legend.show` independent of `GroupVar`, while leaving the rest of your edits alone.

## 2026-07-03T21:02:01.806Z - assistant

A useful detail: `echarts4r::e_pie_()` has its own `legend` argument, separate from the later `e_legend_full()` styling call. I’m going to make Rosetype explicit there and then assert the final legend options after title styling, which is the least invasive way to make slice legends robust.


## 2026-07-03T21:02:20.558Z - assistant

I made Rosetype pass `legend = legend.show` directly into `e_pie_()` and then reassert the final legend `show/data` after title styling. It’s deliberately Rosetype-only and doesn’t touch the shared prep or Pie/Donut.


## 2026-07-03T21:02:20.558Z - tool_call

Topics: autoplots


Tool call: .shell_command
Arguments: {"command":"@'\nsource('R/AccessoryFunctions.R')\nsource('R/revised_echarts4r_functions.R')\nsource('R/theme_helpers.R')\nsource('R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R')\nraw <- data.table::data.table(Category=rep(c('A','B','C'), each=2), Revenue=c(10,12,5,7,20,22))\np <- Rosetype(dt=raw, XVar='Category', YVar='Revenue', PreAgg=FALSE, AggMethod='sum')\ncat('class:', paste(class(p), collapse='/'), '\\n')\ncat('legend.show:', p$x$opts$legend$show, '\\n')\ncat('legend.data:', paste(p$x$opts$legend$data, collapse=', '), '\\n')\ncat('series.names:', paste(vapply(p$x$opts$series[[1]]$data, `[[`, character(1), 'name'), collapse=', '), '\\n')\nq <- Rosetype(dt=raw, XVar='Category', YVar='Revenue', PreAgg=FALSE, AggMethod='sum', legend.show=FALSE)\ncat('legend.false.show:', q$x$opts$legend$show, '\\n')\nstopifnot(inherits(p, 'echarts4r'), isTRUE(p$x$opts$legend$show), identical(p$x$opts$legend$data, c('A','B','C')), identical(q$x$opts$legend$show, FALSE))\ncat('rosetype legend smoke ok\\n')\n'@ | & 'C:\\Program Files\\R\\R-4.2.1\\bin\\Rscript.exe' -","workdir":"C:\\Users\\Bizon\\Documents\\GitHub\\AutoPlots","timeout_ms":120000}

## 2026-07-03T21:03:15.752Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Fixed the Rosetype legend path in [R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R:6337).

What was happening: the widget had legend data, but Rosetype wasn’t passing the legend intent directly into `echarts4r::e_pie_()`. I made that explicit and then reasserted the final legend `show/data` after title styling:

- `legend = legend.show` in the Rosetype `e_pie_()` call
- final `p1$x$opts$legend$show <- TRUE`
- final `p1$x$opts$legend$data <- as.character(temp[[XVar]])`

Verified:
- Rosetype returns `echarts4r/htmlwidget`
- default Rosetype legend has `show = TRUE` and data `A, B, C`
- `legend.show = FALSE` still stays false
- Pie, Donut, Rosetype, Line, Area, Step, and River smoke tests all still pass.


## 2026-07-04T02:29:15.574Z - user

Topics: autoquant_shap, autoplots


Continue the AutoPlots aggregation/prep refactor.

Target file:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R

Goal:
Refactor and clean the shared prep/aggregation logic for Bar and StackedBar.

Rules:
- Modify only R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not modify the Shiny app.
- Do not touch Line, Area, Step, River, Pie, Donut, or Rosetype; those already passed QA.
- Do not touch HeatMap, BarPlot3D, Treemap, Radar, or model-evaluation functions in this task.
- Do not change public function signatures.
- Do not export internal helpers.
- Preserve PreAgg semantics:
  PreAgg = TRUE means input data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
  PreAgg = FALSE means AutoPlots should aggregate internally.
- Preserve existing visual behavior as much as possible.
- Use data.table.
- Avoid dplyr in prep helpers.
- Make the prep logic easier to reason through, not just mechanically extracted.

Current behavior to preserve:
- Bar supports several input shapes:
  1. XVar + YVar
  2. XVar + YVar + GroupVar
  3. YVar + GroupVar, where GroupVar becomes x
  4. XVar + GroupVar, where XVar may be the numeric measure
- StackedBar requires XVar, YVar, and GroupVar.
- If !PreAgg, use SummaryFunction(AggMethod) to aggregate measure vars by grouping vars.
- Numeric/categorical role inference should be preserved.
- Factor/integer/numeric group/category variables are often converted to character for categorical plotting.
- If FacetLevels is supplied and GroupVar exists, filter to those levels.
- If XVar == "Importance" and YVar == "Variable", preserve the existing swap behavior if it exists in Bar.
- LabelValues behavior in Bar should remain unchanged.
- StackedBar should continue to stack by GroupVar.

Implementation:
1. Create an internal helper:

.ap_prep_bar_family_data <- function(
  dt,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  GroupVar = NULL,
  LabelValues = NULL,
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  YVarTrans = "Identity",
  XVarTrans = "Identity",
  FacetLevels = NULL,
  plot_family = c("bar", "stacked_bar"),
  Debug = FALSE
)

Return a list:

list(
  dt = temp,
  XVar = XVar,
  YVar = YVar,
  GroupVar = GroupVar,
  LabelValues = LabelValues,
  numvars = numvars,
  byvars = byvars
)

2. Break helper into smaller helpers if useful.

Suggested helpers:
- .ap_prep_bar_identify_roles()
- .ap_aggregate_bar_family_data()
- .ap_convert_bar_category_vars()
- .ap_apply_bar_transforms()

Reuse existing internal helpers already added where appropriate:
- .ap_not_empty
- .ap_first_or_null
- .ap_clean_var_vector
- .ap_as_dt_copy
- .ap_factor_to_character

Do not duplicate helper definitions if they already exist.

3. Replace Bar prep blocks with .ap_prep_bar_family_data(plot_family = "bar").

4. Replace StackedBar prep blocks with .ap_prep_bar_family_data(plot_family = "stacked_bar").

5. Keep rendering logic separate.
Do not clean visual styling in this task.
Only change rendering where needed to consume prepared data.

6. Add comments for tricky behavior:
- PreAgg = TRUE skips internal aggregation
- role inference exists because XVar/YVar/GroupVar can be numeric or categorical
- categorical axis/group variables are converted to character for plotting
- StackedBar has stricter requirements than Bar

7. Add temporary internal QA helper:

.ap_qa_bar_family_refactor_examples <- function()

It should create fake data and return rendered plots for:
- Bar with XVar + YVar, PreAgg = FALSE, AggMethod = "sum"
- Bar with XVar + YVar + GroupVar, PreAgg = FALSE, AggMethod = "sum"
- Bar with YVar + GroupVar shape if supported
- Bar with PreAgg = TRUE using already aggregated data
- Bar with factor XVar
- StackedBar with XVar + YVar + GroupVar, PreAgg = FALSE
- StackedBar with PreAgg = TRUE using already aggregated data

8. Add temporary internal prepared-data QA helper:

.ap_qa_bar_family_refactor_data <- function()

It should return prepared data outputs from .ap_prep_bar_family_data() for the same representative cases.

9. After changes, source R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R and verify:
- Bar renders with PreAgg = FALSE
- Bar renders with PreAgg = TRUE
- Bar renders with GroupVar
- StackedBar renders with PreAgg = FALSE
- StackedBar renders with PreAgg = TRUE
- Existing Line / Area / Step / River / Pie / Donut / Rosetype examples still source/render

10. Show the diff.

expression(.ap_not_empty <- function(x) {
    !is.null(x) && length(x) > 0L && !all(is.na(x)) && !all(x == 
        "")
}, .ap_first_or_null <- function(x) {
    if (.ap_not_empty(x)) 
        x[1L]
    else NULL
}, .ap_clean_var_vector <- function(x) {
    if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L) 
        return(NULL)
    x <- x[!is.na(x) & x != ""]
    if (length(x) == 0L) 
        NULL
    else x
}, .ap_as_dt_copy <- function(dt) {
    if (!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) {
        dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
    }
    data.table::copy(dt)
}, .ap_factor_to_character <- function(dt, vars) {
    vars <- .ap_clean_var_vector(vars)
    for (var in vars) {
        if (var %in% names(dt) && class(dt[[var]])[1L] == "factor") {
            dt[, `:=`(eval(var), as.character(get(var)))]
        }
    }
    dt
}, .ap_normalize_sequence_vars <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, 
    GroupVar, plot_family) {
    XVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(XVar)
    YVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(YVar)
    DualYVar <- .ap_clean_var_vector(DualYVar)
    GroupVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(GroupVar))
    if (.ap_not_empty(GroupVar) && !.ap_not_empty(XVar) && plot_family %in% 
        c("line", "area", "step")) {
        XVar <- GroupVar
        GroupVar <- NULL
    }
    list(XVar = XVar, YVar = YVar, DualYVar = DualYVar, GroupVar = GroupVar)
}, .ap_validate_sequence_inputs <- function(XVar, YVar, DualYVar, 
    GroupVar, plot_family) {
    has_group <- .ap_not_empty(GroupVar)
    has_dual_y <- .ap_not_empty(DualYVar)
    has_multiple_y <- length(YVar) > 1L
    if (plot_family %in% c("line", "area", "step")) {
        if (has_multiple_y && has_dual_y) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized only one DualYVar is allowed and only one YVar is allowed")
        }
        if (has_group && has_dual_y) {
            stop("When DualYVar is utilized a GroupVar is not allowed")
        }
    }
    invisible(TRUE)
}, .ap_melt_sequence_yvars <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, GroupVar) {

        return(list(dt = data.table::copy(dt), YVar = YVar, GroupVar = GroupVar))
    }
    if (!has_group) {
        dt1 <- data.table::melt.data.table(data = dt, id.vars = XVar,

## 2026-07-04T02:49:54.202Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Continue the AutoPlots aggregation/prep refactor.

Target file:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R

Goal:
Refactor and clean the shared prep/aggregation logic for HeatMap and BarPlot3D.

Rules:
- Modify only R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not modify the Shiny app.
- Do not touch Line, Area, Step, River, Pie, Donut, Rosetype, Bar, or StackedBar; those already passed QA.
- Do not touch Treemap, Radar, model-evaluation functions, or other plot families.
- Do not change public function signatures.
- Do not export internal helpers.
- Preserve PreAgg semantics:
  PreAgg = TRUE means input data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
  PreAgg = FALSE means AutoPlots should aggregate/bin/filter internally.
- Preserve existing visual behavior as much as possible.
- Use data.table.
- Avoid dplyr in prep helpers.
- Make the prep logic easier to reason through, not just mechanically extracted.

Current behavior to preserve:
- HeatMap and BarPlot3D both use XVar, YVar, and ZVar.
- ZVar is the measure/value variable.
- XVar and YVar may be numeric or categorical.
- The code branches based on:
  1. XVar numeric and YVar numeric
  2. XVar categorical and YVar numeric
  3. XVar numeric and YVar categorical
  4. XVar categorical and YVar categorical
- If !PreAgg:
  - numeric X/Y axes may be ranked/binned using NumberBins
  - categorical axes may be reduced to top NumLevels_X / NumLevels_Y levels
  - ZVar is aggregated by XVar/YVar using AggMethod
- If PreAgg:
  - assume data is already prepared enough for plotting
- Factor XVar/YVar should become character.
- Factor/character ZVar may be coerced numeric if current behavior does that.
- HeatMap should still render heatmap.
- BarPlot3D should still render 3D bars.

Implementation:
1. Create an internal helper:

.ap_prep_grid_family_data <- function(
  dt,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  ZVar,
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  XVarTrans = "Identity",
  YVarTrans = "Identity",
  ZVarTrans = "Identity",
  NumberBins = 21,
  NumLevels_X = 33,
  NumLevels_Y = 33,
  plot_family = c("heatmap", "bar3d"),
  Debug = FALSE
)

Return:

list(
  dt = dt1,
  XVar = XVar,
  YVar = YVar,
  ZVar = ZVar,
  value_var = value_var,
  x_is_numeric = x_is_numeric,
  y_is_numeric = y_is_numeric,
  case = case
)

2. Break helper into smaller helpers if useful.

Suggested helpers:
- .ap_grid_axis_case()
- .ap_bin_numeric_grid_axis()
- .ap_top_grid_levels()
- .ap_aggregate_grid_data()
- .ap_prepare_grid_value_var()
- .ap_apply_grid_transforms()

Reuse existing helpers where appropriate:
- .ap_not_empty
- .ap_first_or_null
- .ap_clean_var_vector
- .ap_as_dt_copy
- .ap_factor_to_character

Do not duplicate helper definitions if they already exist.

3. Make the branching explicit.

Use a named case variable:

case <- dplyr-free equivalent / base R:
- "both_numeric"
- "x_categorical_y_numeric"
- "x_numeric_y_categorical"
- "both_categorical"

Do not use dplyr.

4. Replace HeatMap prep logic with .ap_prep_grid_family_data(plot_family = "heatmap").

5. Replace BarPlot3D prep logic with .ap_prep_grid_family_data(plot_family = "bar3d").

6. Keep rendering logic separate.
Do not do visual styling cleanup in this task.
Only change rendering where needed to consume prepared data.

7. Add comments for tricky behavior:
- PreAgg = TRUE skips internal aggregation/binning
- numeric axes are binned/ranked to reduce dense continuous grids
- categorical axes are top-N filtered before grid aggregation
- ZVar is renamed/prepared as the grid value variable where current behavior requires it

8. Add temporary internal QA helper:

.ap_qa_grid_family_refactor_examples <- function()

It should create fake data and return rendered plots for:

HeatMap:
- both categorical axes, PreAgg = FALSE
- X categorical / Y numeric, PreAgg = FALSE
- X numeric / Y categorical, PreAgg = FALSE
- both numeric axes, PreAgg = FALSE
- PreAgg = TRUE with already aggregated data

BarPlot3D:
- both categorical axes, PreAgg = FALSE
- X categorical / Y numeric, PreAgg = FALSE
- X numeric / Y categorical, PreAgg = FALSE
- both numeric axes, PreAgg = FALSE
- PreAgg = TRUE with already aggregated data

9. Add temporary internal prepared-data QA helper:

.ap_qa_grid_family_refactor_data <- function()

It should return prepared data outputs from .ap_prep_grid_family_data() for the same representative cases.

10. After changes, source R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R and verify:
- HeatMap renders in all four X/Y type cases
- HeatMap renders with PreAgg = TRUE
- BarPlot3D renders in all four X/Y type cases
- BarPlot3D renders with PreAgg = TRUE
- Existing Line / Area / Step / River / Pie / Donut / Rosetype / Bar / StackedBar examples still source/render

11. Show the diff.

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })

  # Convert factor to character
  if(length(ZVar) > 0L && class(dt[[ZVar]])[1L] %in% c("factor","character")) {
    dt[, eval(ZVar) := as.numeric(get(ZVar))]
  }
  if(length(XVar) > 0L && class(dt[[XVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(XVar) := as.character(get(XVar))]
  }
  if(length(YVar) > 0L && class(dt[[YVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(YVar) := as.character(get(YVar))]
  }

  # Subset cols
  dt1 <- dt[, .SD, .SDcols = c(XVar,YVar,ZVar)]
  x_check <- class(dt1[[XVar]])[1L] %in% c('numeric','integer')
  y_check <- class(dt1[[YVar]])[1L] %in% c('numeric','integer')
  x_y_num <- x_check && y_check
  x_num <- x_check && !y_check
  x_char <- !x_check && y_check
  all_char <- !x_check && !y_check


  Z.HoverFormat <- "%{zaxis.title.text}: %{y:,.2f}<br>"

  TimeLine <- FALSE
  if(TimeLine && length(FacetLevels) > 0) X_Scroll <- FALSE

  if(!PreAgg) {
    aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  }

  # XVar == numeric or integer && YVar == numeric or integer
  if(x_y_num) {

    # rank XVar and YVar
    if(!PreAgg) {
      dt1[, eval(XVar) := round(data.table::frank(dt1[[XVar]]) * NumberBins /.N) / NumberBins]
      dt1[, eval(YVar) := round(data.table::frank(dt1[[YVar]]) * NumberBins /.N) / NumberBins]
      data.table::setnames(dt1, eval(ZVar), 'Measure_Variable')
      dt1 <- dt1[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = c(ZVar), by = c(XVar,YVar)]
    }

    # Transformation
    if(ZVarTrans != "Identity") {
      dt1 <- AutoTransformationCreate(data = dt1, ColumnNames = "Measure_Variable", Methods = ZVarTrans)$Data
    }

    # Formatting
    vals <- unique(scales::rescale(c(dt1[['Measure_Variable']])))
    o <- order(vals, decreasing = FALSE)
    cols <- scales::col_numeric("Purples", domain = NULL)(vals)
    colz <- stats::setNames(data.frame(vals[o], cols[o]), NULL)

    # Create final data for plot

    p1 <- echarts4r::e_charts_(
      data = dt1,
      x = XVar,

                      toolbox.feature.magicType.type = c("line", "bar", "stack"),
                      toolbox.feature.dataView.show = TRUE,

                      Debug = FALSE) {

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })

  # Convert factor to character
  if(length(ZVar) > 0L && class(dt[[ZVar]])[1L] %in% c("factor","character")) {
    dt[, eval(ZVar) := as.numeric(get(ZVar))]
  }
  if(length(XVar) > 0L && class(dt[[XVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(XVar) := as.character(get(XVar))]
  }
  if(length(YVar) > 0L && class(dt[[YVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(YVar) := as.character(get(YVar))]
  }

  # Subset cols
  dt1 <- dt[, .SD, .SDcols = c(XVar,YVar,ZVar)]
  x_check <- class(dt1[[XVar]])[1L] %in% c('numeric','integer')
  y_check <- class(dt1[[YVar]])[1L] %in% c('numeric','integer')
  x_y_num <- x_check && y_check
  x_num <- x_check && !y_check
  x_char <- !x_check && y_check
  all_char <- !x_check && !y_check


  Z.HoverFormat <- "%{zaxis.title.text}: %{y:,.2f}<br>"

  TimeLine <- FALSE
  if(TimeLine && length(FacetLevels) > 0) X_Scroll <- FALSE

  if(!PreAgg) {
    aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  }

  # XVar == numeric or integer && YVar == numeric or integer
  if(x_y_num) {

    # rank XVar and YVar
    if(!PreAgg) {
      dt1[, eval(XVar) := round(data.table::frank(dt1[[XVar]]) * NumberBins /.N) / NumberBins]
      dt1[, eval(YVar) := round(data.table::frank(dt1[[YVar]]) * NumberBins /.N) / NumberBins]
      data.table::setnames(dt1, eval(ZVar), 'Measure_Variable')
      dt1 <- dt1[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = c(ZVar), by = c(XVar,YVar)]
    }

  apply_theme_defaults(Theme, plot_type = "HeatMap", grouped = FALSE, env = environment())

  if(!data.table::is.data.table(dt)) tryCatch({data.table::setDT(dt)}, error = function(x) {
    dt <- data.table::as.data.table(dt)
  })

  # Convert factor to character
  if(length(ZVar) > 0L && class(dt[[ZVar]])[1L] %in% c("factor","character")) {

  }

  }
  if(length(YVar) > 0L && class(dt[[YVar]])[1L] == "factor") {
    dt[, eval(YVar) := as.character(get(YVar))]
  }

  # Subset cols

      dt1[, eval(XVar) := round(data.table::frank(dt1[[XVar]]) * NumberBins /.N) / NumberBins]
      dt1[, eval(YVar) := round(data.table::frank(dt1[[YVar]]) * NumberBins /.N) / NumberBins]
    }

    # Transformation
    if(ZVarTrans != "Identity") {
      dt1 <- AutoTransformationCreate(data = dt1, ColumnNames = ZVar, Methods = ZVarTrans)$Data
    }

    # Formatting
    vals <- unique(scales::rescale(c(dt1[[ZVar]])))
    o <- order(vals, decreasing = FALSE)
    cols <- scales::col_numeric("Purples", domain = NULL)(vals)
    colz <- stats::setNames(data.frame(vals[o], cols[o]), NULL)
    data.table::setnames(dt1, ZVar, "Measure_Variable")
    data.table::setorderv(x = dt1, cols = c(XVar,YVar),c(1L,1L))

    # Create final data for plot
    g <- "Measure_Variable"
    p1 <- echarts4r::e_charts_(
      data = dt1,
      x = XVar,
      darkMode = TRUE,
      width = Width,
      height = Height)

    p1 <- e_heatmap_full(
      e = p1, y = YVar, z = ZVar,

.ap_grid_axis_case <- function(dt, XVar, YVar) {
  x_is_numeric <- class(dt[[XVar]])[1L] %in% c("numeric", "integer")
  y_is_numeric <- class(dt[[YVar]])[1L] %in% c("numeric", "integer")

  case <- if (x_is_numeric && y_is_numeric) {
    "both_numeric"
  } else if (!x_is_numeric && y_is_numeric) {
    "x_categorical_y_numeric"
  } else if (x_is_numeric && !y_is_numeric) {
    "x_numeric_y_categorical"
  } else {
    "both_categorical"
  }

  list(case = case, x_is_numeric = x_is_numeric, y_is_numeric = y_is_numeric)
}

.ap_bin_numeric_grid_axis <- function(dt, var, NumberBins) {
  # Numeric axes are binned/ranked to reduce dense continuous grids.
  if (.ap_not_empty(var) && var %in% names(dt)) {
    dt[, eval(var) := round(data.table::frank(get(var)) * NumberBins / .N) / NumberBins]
  }
  dt
}

.ap_top_grid_levels <- function(dt, category_var, value_var, n_levels) {
  if (!.ap_not_empty(category_var) || !category_var %in% names(dt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Categorical axes are top-N filtered before grid aggregation.
  top_levels <- dt[
    ,
    lapply(.SD, mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .SDcols = value_var,
    by = category_var
  ][order(-get(value_var))]

  top_levels[seq_len(min(n_levels, top_levels[, .N]))][[category_var]]
}

.ap_aggregate_grid_data <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, value_var, AggMethod) {
  aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  dt[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = value_var, by = c(XVar, YVar)]
}

## 2026-07-04T03:14:46.719Z - user

Topics: ux


Fix HeatMap categorical/numeric grid rendering after the grid prep refactor.

Target file:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R

Problem:
HeatMap with X categorical and Y numeric renders axes/tooltips/visualMap, but no visible heatmap cells.

Failing visual case:

dt <- data.table::data.table(
  XCat = sample(LETTERS[1:8], 2000, TRUE),
  YNum = rnorm(2000, 50, 10),
  ZVal = runif(2000, 10, 500)
)

HeatMap(
  dt = dt,
  XVar = "XCat",
  YVar = "YNum",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "HeatMap Cat / Num"
)

The data appears in tooltip, but heatmap cells are not visible.

Likely cause:
For HeatMap, numeric X/Y axes are currently ranked/binned to decimal values like 0.04761904761904762. ECharts heatmap behaves more reliably when both heatmap axes are categorical coordinates. The prep helper should turn binned numeric axes into clean category labels for HeatMap.

Rules:
- Modify only R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not touch Line, Area, Step, River, Pie, Donut, Rosetype, Bar, or StackedBar.
- Do not change public function signatures.
- Preserve PreAgg semantics.
- Preserve BarPlot3D behavior unless the same fix is clearly required there.
- Keep data.table.

Required fix:
1. In the grid prep helper, after numeric axis binning for HeatMap, convert binned numeric XVar/YVar plotting columns to character or factor-like category labels.

Example:
Instead of YVar values:
0.04761904761904762

Use clean labels:
"0.048"
or preferably ordered labels:
"bin_001", "bin_002", ... with a readable display label if already supported.

Simplest acceptable fix:
- round binned numeric axis values to a reasonable number of digits, e.g. 4 or 6
- convert to character
- preserve ordering with setorderv before conversion where needed

2. Apply this for HeatMap cases:
- both_numeric
- x_categorical_y_numeric
- x_numeric_y_categorical

3. Ensure the prepared dt contains:
- XVar
- YVar
- value_var

4. Ensure HeatMap rendering uses value_var returned by prep.

5. Add a helper if useful:

.ap_format_heatmap_axis_bins <- function(dt, vars, digits = 4)

It should:
- only touch vars that exist
- round numeric values
- convert to character
- keep ordered category-like labels

6. Add debug/QA case to .ap_qa_grid_family_refactor_examples() and .ap_qa_grid_family_refactor_data():
- HeatMap X categorical / Y numeric
- HeatMap X numeric / Y categorical
- HeatMap X numeric / Y numeric

  dt = dt,
  XVar = "XCat",
  YVar = "YNum",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,

)

  YVar = "YNum",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "HeatMap Num / Num"
)

All should visibly render heatmap cells, not just axes/tooltips.

.ap_bin_numeric_grid_axis <- function(dt, var, NumberBins) {
  # Numeric axes are binned/ranked to reduce dense continuous grids.
  if (.ap_not_empty(var) && var %in% names(dt)) {
    dt[, eval(var) := round(data.table::frank(get(var)) * NumberBins / .N) / NumberBins]
  }
  dt
}

.ap_top_grid_levels <- function(dt, category_var, value_var, n_levels) {
  if (!.ap_not_empty(category_var) || !category_var %in% names(dt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Categorical axes are top-N filtered before grid aggregation.
  top_levels <- dt[
    ,
    lapply(.SD, mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .SDcols = value_var,
    by = category_var
  ][order(-get(value_var))]

  top_levels[seq_len(min(n_levels, top_levels[, .N]))][[category_var]]
}

.ap_aggregate_grid_data <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, value_var, AggMethod) {
  aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  dt[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = value_var, by = c(XVar, YVar)]
}

.ap_prepare_grid_value_var <- function(dt, ZVar, value_var = "Measure_Variable") {
  if (class(dt[[ZVar]])[1L] %in% c("factor", "character")) {
    dt[, eval(ZVar) := as.numeric(get(ZVar))]
  }
  if (!identical(ZVar, value_var)) {
    dt[, eval(value_var) := get(ZVar)]
  }
  dt
}

.ap_apply_grid_transforms <- function(dt, value_var, ZVarTrans) {
  if (length(ZVarTrans) > 0L && ZVarTrans != "Identity") {
    dt <- AutoTransformationCreate(data = dt, ColumnNames = value_var, Methods = ZVarTrans)$Data
  }
  dt
}

.ap_prep_grid_family_data <- function(
  dt,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  ZVar,
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  XVarTrans = "Identity",
  YVarTrans = "Identity",
  ZVarTrans = "Identity",
  NumberBins = 21,
  NumLevels_X = 33,
  NumLevels_Y = 33,
  plot_family = c("heatmap", "bar3d"),
  Debug = FALSE
) {
  plot_family <- match.arg(plot_family)

  XVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(XVar))
  YVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(YVar))
  ZVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(ZVar))
  if (!.ap_not_empty(XVar) || !.ap_not_empty(YVar) || !.ap_not_empty(ZVar)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value_var <- "Measure_Variable"
  dt1 <- .ap_as_dt_copy(dt)
  dt1 <- .ap_factor_to_character(dt1, c(XVar, YVar))
  dt1 <- dt1[, .SD, .SDcols = c(XVar, YVar, ZVar)]
  dt1 <- .ap ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-04T03:25:30.424Z - user

Topics: ux


Fix BarPlot3D numeric-axis rendering and axis-label formatting after the grid prep refactor.

Target file:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R

Problem:
BarPlot3D works for categorical X / categorical Y, but fails visually for categorical X / numeric Y.

Example:

p_bar3d_cat_num <- BarPlot3D(
  dt = dt3d,
  XVar = "XCat",
  YVar = "YNum",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Bar3D Cat / Num"
)

The plot renders axes but no visible bars. The numeric-binned axis also displays ugly raw decimal labels like:
0.04761904761904762

Rules:
- Modify only R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not touch Line, Area, Step, River, Pie, Donut, Rosetype, Bar, StackedBar, or HeatMap unless absolutely necessary.
- Do not change public function signatures.
- Preserve PreAgg semantics.
- Keep data.table.
- Do not modify the Shiny app.

Likely cause:
The grid prep helper fixed HeatMap numeric-axis binning/formatting, but BarPlot3D still has one or more of these issues:
1. numeric axis bins are not converted to clean category labels for BarPlot3D
2. prepared dt does not contain the returned XVar/YVar/value_var columns
3. BarPlot3D rendering still uses the original ZVar instead of prep$value_var
4. e_bar_3d_ is receiving inconsistent axis/value columns after prep

Required changes:

1. In .ap_prep_grid_family_data(), ensure BarPlot3D also receives clean formatted axis values for numeric-binned axes.

For BarPlot3D cases:
- both_numeric
- x_categorical_y_numeric
- x_numeric_y_categorical

After binning, convert numeric binned axes to clean character labels.

Use a helper if available from the HeatMap fix, such as:
.ap_format_heatmap_axis_bins()

If that helper exists, either generalize its name or reuse it for BarPlot3D too.

Preferred name:
.ap_format_grid_axis_bins()

Behavior:
- only touch vars that exist
- if a var is numeric, round to a reasonable number of digits, e.g. 4
- convert to character
- preserve ordering before conversion where needed
- avoid labels like 0.04761904761904762

2. Make BarPlot3D rendering use prep$value_var consistently.

After prep, BarPlot3D should use:
- XVar <- prep$XVar
- YVar <- prep$YVar
- value_var <- prep$value_var
- dt1 <- prep$dt

Then e_bar_3d_ should use value_var as the Z/value column.

3. Add defensive validation after prep for BarPlot3D:

missing_cols <- setdiff(c(XVar, YVar, value_var), names(dt1))
if (length(missing_cols)) {
  stop(
    "Grid prep output is missing required columns: ",
    paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "),
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

4. Improve 3D axis label formatting.

For BarPlot3D numeric-binned axes, axis labels should not show raw long decimals.

If the binned values are converted to character, the visible axis labels should be short.

If numeric values remain numeric for any 3D axis, add/adjust axis label formatter or round the data before rendering.

5. Add/update QA helpers.

Update .ap_qa_grid_family_refactor_examples() and .ap_qa_grid_family_refactor_data() to include:

BarPlot3D:
- categorical X / categorical Y
- categorical X / numeric Y
- numeric X / categorical Y
- numeric X / numeric Y

set.seed(8675309)

dt3d <- data.table::data.table(
  XCat = sample(LETTERS[1:8], 2000, TRUE),
  YCat = sample(paste0("Group_", 1:8), 2000, TRUE),
  XNum = rnorm(2000, 100, 25),
  YNum = rnorm(2000, 50, 10),
  ZVal = runif(2000, 10, 500)
)

p_bar3d_cat_cat <- BarPlot3D(
  dt = dt3d,
  XVar = "XCat",
  YVar = "YCat",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Bar3D Cat / Cat"
)

p_bar3d_cat_num <- BarPlot3D(
  dt = dt3d,
  XVar = "XCat",
  YVar = "YNum",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Bar3D Cat / Num"
)

p_bar3d_num_cat <- BarPlot3D(
  dt = dt3d,
  XVar = "XNum",
  YVar = "YCat",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Bar3D Num / Cat"
)

p_bar3d_num_num <- BarPlot3D(
  dt = dt3d,
  XVar = "XNum",
  YVar = "YNum",
  ZVar = "ZVal",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Bar3D Num / Num"
)

All should:
- return htmlwidget objects
- visibly render bars
- avoid long decimal axis labels

.ap_bin_numeric_grid_axis <- function(dt, var, NumberBins) {
  # Numeric axes are binned/ranked to reduce dense continuous grids.
  if (.ap_not_empty(var) && var %in% names(dt)) {
    dt[, eval(var) := round(data.table::frank(get(var)) * NumberBins / .N) / NumberBins]
  }
  dt
}

.ap_format_heatmap_axis_bins <- function(dt, vars, digits = 4) {
  for (var in vars) {
    if (.ap_not_empty(var) && var %in% names(dt) && is.numeric(dt[[var]])) {
      vals <- round(dt[[var]], digits)
      dt[, eval(var) := format(vals, trim = TRUE, scientific = FALSE)]
    }
  }
  dt
}

.ap_top_grid_levels <- function(dt, category_var, value_var, n_levels) {
  if (!.ap_not_empty(category_var) || !category_var %in% names(dt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Categorical axes are top-N filtered before grid aggregation.
  top_levels <- dt[
    ,
    lapply(.SD, mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .SDcols = value_var,
    by = category_var
  ][order(-get(value_var))]

  top_levels[seq_len(min(n_levels, top_levels[, .N]))][[category_var]]
}

.ap_aggregate_grid_data <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, value_var, AggMethod) {
  aggFunc <- SummaryFunction(AggMethod)
  dt[, lapply(.SD, noquote(aggFunc)), .SDcols = value_var, by = c(XVar, YVar)]
}

.ap_prepare_grid_value_var <- function(dt, ZVar, value_var = "Measure_Variable") {
  if (class(dt[[ZVar]])[1L] %in% c("factor", "character")) {
    dt[, eval(ZVar) := as.numeric(get(ZVar))]
  }
  if (!identical(ZVar, value_var)) {
    dt[, eval(value_var) := get(ZVar)]
  }
  dt
}

.ap_apply_grid_transforms <- function(dt, value_var, ZVarTrans) {
  if (length(ZVarTrans) > 0L && ZVarTrans != "Identity") {
    dt <- AutoTransformationCreate(data = dt, ColumnNames = value_var, Methods = ZVarTrans)$Data
  }
  dt
}

.ap_prep_grid_family_data <- function(
  dt,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  ZVar,
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "mean",
  XVarTrans = "Identity",
  YVarTrans = "Identity",
  ZVarTrans = "Identity",
  NumberBins = 21,
  NumLevels_X = 33,
  NumLevels_Y = 33,
  plot_family = c("heatmap", "bar3d"),
  Debug = FALSE
) {
  plot_family <- match.arg(plot_family)

  XVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(XVar))
  YVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(YVar))
  ZVar <- .ap_first_or_null(.ap_clean_var_vector(ZVar))
  if (!.ap_not_empty(XVar) || !.ap_not_empty(YVar) || !.ap_not_empty(ZVar)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value_var <- "Measure_Variable"
  dt1 <- .ap_as_dt_copy(dt)
  dt1 <- .ap_factor_to_character(dt1, c(XVar, YVar ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-04T03:27:57.054Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Fixed the BarPlot3D numeric-axis rendering issue in [R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R).

What changed:
- Generalized `.ap_format_heatmap_axis_bins()` to `.ap_format_grid_axis_bins()`.
- Applied clean binned-axis labels to both `HeatMap` and `BarPlot3D`.
- Updated the mixed BarPlot3D branches:
  - categorical X / numeric Y
  - numeric X / categorical Y
- Those branches now call `echarts4r::e_bar_3d_()` instead of `echarts4r::e_heatmap_()`.
- BarPlot3D continues to use `prep$value_var` consistently.

Verified after sourcing:
- `BarPlot3D` cat/cat, cat/num, num/cat, and num/num all return `echarts4r/htmlwidget`.
- Prepared numeric binned axes are character labels like `"0.0476"`, not long raw decimals.
- HeatMap cat/num, num/cat, and num/num still return widgets.

`devtools::load_all(".", reset = TRUE, export_all = FALSE)` is still blocked locally because the package dependency `webshot2` is missing.

Diff summary:

```text
R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R | 2563 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++-----------------------
1 file changed, 1388 insertions(+), 1175 deletions(-)
```


## 2026-07-04T04:05:06.487Z - user

Topics: ux


Polish 3D axis formatting for BarPlot3D and Scatter3D and Copula3D. In the BarPlot3D I manually removed a call to e_x_axis_full and e_y_axis full since those aren't for 3D plots and there was a call to them in one of the branches.  

Target file:
R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R

Goal:
Improve default axis label readability for 3D plots, especially numeric-binned axes and long decimal labels.

Rules:
- Modify only R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not change public function signatures.
- Do not change aggregation/prep behavior.
- Do not touch 2D plot families.
- Preserve existing visual behavior except for cleaner 3D axis labels.
- Use small targeted changes.

Tasks:
1. Inspect BarPlot3D and Scatter3D axis configuration.
2. Add or reuse an internal helper for 3D axis label formatting if appropriate.
3. Avoid long labels like 0.04761904761904762 on axes.
4. For numeric-binned axes, round labels to a reasonable number of digits.
5. Make axis titles and axis labels readable under dark themes.
6. Ensure BarPlot3D still renders:
   - categorical / categorical
   - categorical / numeric
   - numeric / categorical
   - numeric / numeric
7. Ensure Scatter3D still renders with numeric X/Y/Z and optional GroupVar.
8. Do not alter HeatMap.
9. Show the diff.

## 2026-07-04T04:05:54.659Z - assistant

I’m going to add a small internal `.ap_apply_3d_axis_defaults()` helper that writes `xAxis3D`, `yAxis3D`, and `zAxis3D` options directly. That gives BarPlot3D, Scatter3D, and Copula3D the same readable 3D labels without changing any public function signatures.


## 2026-07-04T04:06:18.504Z - assistant

The shared helper is in place. Now I’m wiring it into BarPlot3D’s four return paths, then I’ll replace the remaining 2D axis calls in Scatter3D/Copula3D with this 3D helper.

## 2026-07-04T04:11:35.587Z - assistant

Topics: ux, autoplots


Implemented the 3D axis label polish in [R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R).

What changed:
- Added internal `.ap_apply_3d_axis_defaults()`.
- Applies `xAxis3D`, `yAxis3D`, and `zAxis3D` styling directly to the htmlwidget.
- Adds a JS formatter that rounds normal numeric labels to 4 digits, uses scientific notation for very small/large values, and truncates long category labels.
- Uses readable dark-theme defaults for axis titles, labels, axis lines, and split lines.
- Wired the helper into:
  - all four `BarPlot3D` branch returns
  - `Copula3D`
  - `Scatter3D`

Verified:
- `BarPlot3D` categorical/categorical, categorical/numeric, numeric/categorical, numeric/numeric all return `echarts4r/htmlwidget`.
- `Scatter3D` renders with numeric X/Y/Z and with optional `GroupVar`.
- `Copula3D` renders with and without `GroupVar`.
- Confirmed `xAxis3D/yAxis3D/zAxis3D` options now include the label formatter and dark label color.

Note: I left HeatMap untouched for this pass.

.ap_apply_3d_axis_defaults <- function(
  e,
  XVar,
  YVar,
  ZVar,
  xAxis.title = NULL,
  yAxis.title = NULL,
  zAxis.title = NULL,
  Theme = "dark"
) {
  dark_theme <- length(Theme) > 0L && grepl("dark", Theme[1L], ignore.case = TRUE)
  label_color <- if (dark_theme) "#E5E7EB" else "#374151"
  line_color <- if (dark_theme) "#6B7280" else "#9CA3AF"
  split_color <- if (dark_theme) "rgba(229,231,235,0.16)" else "rgba(55,65,81,0.16)"
  formatter <- htmlwidgets::JS(
    "function(value) {
       var text = String(value);
       var number = Number(value);
       if (text !== '' && isFinite(number)) {
         var abs = Math.abs(number);
         if (abs !== 0 && (abs < 0.001 || abs >= 10000)) {
           return number.toExponential(2);
         }
         return Number(number.toFixed(4)).toString();
       }
       return text.length > 18 ? text.slice(0, 17) + '...' : text;
     }"
  )

  axis_opts <- function(name) {
    list(
      name = name,
      nameGap = 24,
      nameTextStyle = list(
        color = label_color,
        fontFamily = "Segoe UI",
        fontSize = 13,
        fontWeight = "bold"
      ),
      axisLabel = list(
        color = label_color,
        fontFamily = "Segoe UI",
        fontSize = 11,
        formatter = formatter
      ),
      axisLine = list(lineStyle = list(color = line_color)),
      axisPointer = list(label = list(formatter = formatter)),
      splitLine = list(lineStyle = list(color = split_color))
    )
  }

  e$x$opts$xAxis3D <- axis_opts(if (length(xAxis.title) > 0L) xAxis.title else XVar)
  e$x$opts$yAxis3D <- axis_opts(if (length(yAxis.title) > 0L) yAxis.title else YVar)
  e$x$opts$zAxis3D <- axis_opts(if (length(zAxis.title) > 0L) zAxis.title else ZVar)
  e
}

.ap_top_grid_levels <- function(dt, category_var, value_var, n_levels) {
  if (!.ap_not_empty(category_var) || !category_var %in% names(dt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Categorical axes are top-N filtered before grid aggregation.
  top_levels <- dt[
    ,
    lapply(.SD, mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .SDcols = value_var,
    by = category_var
  ][order(-get(value_var))]

  top_levels[seq_len(min(n_levels, top_levels[, .N]))][[category_var]]
}

.ap_aggregate_grid_data <- function(dt, XVar, YVar, value_var, AggMet ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-04T04:23:32.086Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Good catch. The axis polish helper was still too aggressive: it preserved the series data, but BarPlot3D also needs explicit category axis metadata for prepared string coordinates like `XCat` and binned `"0.0476"` values.

I fixed [R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R) so:

- `.ap_apply_3d_axis_defaults()` now merges into existing 3D axis config instead of replacing it.
- BarPlot3D passes prepared `dt1[[XVar]]` and `dt1[[YVar]]` into the helper.
- If those axis values are character, the helper sets `type = "category"` and `data = unique(values)`.
- Series data remains intact.

Verified for `BarPlot3D(XCat, YNum, ZVal)`:
- widget returns successfully
- `xAxis3D$type == "category"`
- `yAxis3D$type == "category"`
- axis data is populated
- series data is populated (`176` points in my smoke test)
- labels remain clean (`"0.0476"` style)

I also reran the broader 3D smoke for BarPlot3D, Scatter3D, and Copula3D, including grouped variants.

## 2026-07-04T04:39:23.572Z - assistant

The inspection shows the exact serialization problem: existing `xAxis3D` is `list(list(type=..., data=...))`, and my merge added named fields beside that unnamed first element. That creates the “fully named list” error. I’m flattening any unnamed existing 3D axis sublist before merging so the final option list is fully named.

## 2026-07-04T04:40:57.503Z - assistant

Fixed that serialization bug too. The issue was an unnamed nested 3D axis option from `echarts4r` getting merged with named options, which caused:

```text
'options' must be a fully named list, or have no names (NULL)
```

I updated `.ap_apply_3d_axis_defaults()` so it flattens unnamed existing axis sublists before merging. Verified locally:

- `BarPlot3D` cat/cat serializes cleanly with `htmlwidgets:::toJSON()`
- no unnamed fields remain in `xAxis3D`, `yAxis3D`, or `zAxis3D`
- category axis metadata is preserved
- series data is still populated
- full 3D smoke passes for BarPlot3D, Scatter3D, and Copula3D, including grouped variants

This should fix `p_bar3d_cat_cat` printing directly in the console.


## 2026-07-04T04:47:24.480Z - user

Topics: ux, autoplots


Alrighty, now that we've refactored the data aggregation stuff, we can move back to the app. Expose AutoPlots built-in aggregation controls in the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to use AutoPlots built-in PreAgg/AggMethod behavior from the plot builder.

Rules:
- Do not implement app-side data.table aggregation in this task.
- Do not modify AutoPlots in this task.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven UI.
- Make small targeted changes.

Important AutoPlots semantics:
PreAgg = TRUE means the data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
PreAgg = FALSE means AutoPlots should aggregate internally.

Therefore the UI should NOT expose a raw "PreAgg" checkbox.

Add user-facing controls:
- Auto-aggregate before plotting
- AggMethod

Implementation requirements:

1. Add option_registry entries for:
- AutoAggregate
- AggMethod

AutoAggregate:
- input_id = "auto_aggregate"
- label = "Auto-aggregate before plotting"
- type = "checkbox"
- default = FALSE

AggMethod:
- input_id = "agg_method"
- label = "AggMethod"
- type = "select"
- choices = c("mean", "sum", "median", "sd", "min", "max", "count")
- default = "mean"

2. Add AutoAggregate and AggMethod to plot_registry options only for plot types that currently support PreAgg/AggMethod in the app.

Add to:
- Line
- Area
- Step if present
- River if present
- Bar
- StackedBar if present
- Pie
- Donut
- Rosetype if present
- HeatMap
- BarPlot3D if present
- Radar if present
- Treemap if present

Do not add to:
- Histogram
- Density
- Scatter
- CorrMatrix
- Box
- WordCloud
- Parallel

3. Update option rendering:
- AggMethod should only be visible when AutoAggregate is TRUE if that is simple with the current dynamic UI.
- If conditional visibility is annoying, it is acceptable for AggMethod to always show for supported plot types, but generated args should still be correct.

4. Update option_value/build_plot_args.

Special handling:
- AutoAggregate should not become an argument named AutoAggregate.
- It should produce PreAgg.

If input$auto_aggregate is TRUE:
  args$PreAgg <- FALSE

If input$auto_aggregate is FALSE:
  args$PreAgg <- TRUE

AggMethod should produce:
  args$AggMethod <- input$agg_method

5. Generated code should show actual AutoPlots arguments.

Example:

Line(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "Date",
  YVar = "Spend",
  GroupVar = "Channel",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "sum",
  Theme = "dark"
)

6. Build / Refresh Plot should pass PreAgg and AggMethod into the AutoPlots function.

7. Smoke tests:
- Line with Auto-aggregate unchecked generates PreAgg = TRUE.
- Line with Auto-aggregate checked and AggMethod = sum generates PreAgg = FALSE, AggMethod = "sum".
- Line renders with duplicated transactional data after one click.
- Bar renders with Auto-aggregate checked.
- Pie renders with Auto-aggregate checked.
- HeatMap renders with Auto-aggregate checked.
- Histogram does not show Auto-aggregate controls.
- Scatter does not show Auto-aggregate controls.
- CorrMatrix does not show Auto-aggregate controls.
- Generated code remains valid.

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

validate_plot_config_ready <- function(config, data) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return("No data is available.")
  }

  spec <- plot_spec(config$plot_type)

  for (mapping in spec$mappings) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]

    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || any(value == "")) {
      return(paste0("Required mapping is missing: ", mapping))
    }

    if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))
      if (length(missing_cols)) {
        return(paste0(
          "CorrVars contains columns not in data: ",
          paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")
        ))
      }
    } else if (!value %in% names(data)) {
      return(paste0(mapping, " column is not in data: ", value))
    }
  }

  TRUE
}

arg_to_code <- function(name, value) {
  if (is.logical(value) || is.numeric(value)) {
    return(paste0(name, " = ", deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)))
  }

  paste0(name, " = ", r_string(value))
}

build_autoplots_code <- function(plot_type, input) {
  args <- build_plot_args(plot_type, data = NULL, input = input, include_data = FALSE)
  arg_lines <- c(
    "dt = data",
    unlist(Map(arg_to_code, names(args), args), use.names = FALSE)
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    "p1 <- AutoPlots::", plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(arg_lines, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "p1"
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

## 2026-07-04T04:50:19.478Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Implemented in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Added `AutoAggregate` and `AggMethod` to the option registry.
- Added those controls only to `Area`, `Line`, `Bar`, `Pie`, `Donut`, and `HeatMap`.
- Left `Histogram`, `Density`, `Scatter`, and `CorrMatrix` without aggregation controls.
- Converted UI intent into AutoPlots args:
  - unchecked `Auto-aggregate before plotting` -> `PreAgg = TRUE`
  - checked -> `PreAgg = FALSE`
  - `AggMethod` passes through as a flat `AggMethod = "..."` argument
- Updated both live plot builds and generated R code to use the same registry-driven conversion.

Verified with R smoke tests:
- Generated code shows `PreAgg = TRUE` when unchecked.
- Generated code shows `PreAgg = FALSE, AggMethod = "sum"` when checked.
- `AutoAggregate` does not leak into AutoPlots arguments.
- Line, Bar, Pie, and HeatMap all returned `htmlwidget` objects with auto aggregation enabled.
- Unsupported plot types do not include the new controls.

One note: `app.R` is currently untracked in git, so `git diff -- app.R` has nothing to compare against.


## 2026-07-04T06:14:47.835Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add multi-plot support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to save the currently built plot into a plot list, then build additional plots.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven plot options.
- Do not implement layout/display_plots_sections yet.
- Do not implement export yet.
- Make small targeted changes.

Requirements:

1. Add reactiveValues for saved plots.

saved_plots <- reactiveValues(
  plots = list(),
  configs = list(),
  code = list()
)

2. Add an action button near Build / Refresh Plot:

actionButton("add_plot", "Add Plot", class = "btn-success")

3. When Add Plot is clicked:
- require a successfully built current plot
- assign next plot name: p1, p2, p3, etc.
- store the plot object
- store the current plot config snapshot
- store the generated code for that plot
- show a friendly message like "Added p1"

4. Add a plot list panel.

Show saved plots in a table or simple UI list with:
- plot name
- plot type
- title.text if available
- XVar
- YVar
- GroupVar if available

5. Add ability to remove a saved plot.

Simple approach:
- Add "Remove Last Plot" button first.
- Do not implement arbitrary delete/reorder yet unless easy.

6. Add a preview of saved plots.

In the Layout tab, show saved plots one after another using htmltools::tagList.

For now, do not use display_plots_grid() or display_plots_sections().
Just render the saved plot objects in order.

7. Update generated code.

Add a second code output for all saved plots.

It should generate:

library(AutoPlots)

data <- data.table::fread("path/to/data.csv")

p1 <- AutoPlots::<PlotType>(...)
p2 <- AutoPlots::<PlotType>(...)
p3 <- AutoPlots::<PlotType>(...)

list(p1, p2, p3)

8. Existing single-plot generated code should remain.

9. Smoke tests:
- Build a Line plot and Add Plot. It appears as p1.
- Build a Bar plot and Add Plot. It appears as p2.
- Layout tab shows p1 and p2.
- Saved plots do not change when current controls change.
- Generated all-plots code includes p1 and p2.
- Remove Last Plot removes p2.

validate_plot_config_ready <- function(config, data) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return("No data is available.")
  }

  spec <- plot_spec(config$plot_type)

  for (mapping in spec$mappings) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]

    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || any(value == "")) {
      return(paste0("Required mapping is missing: ", mapping))
    }

    if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))
      if (length(missing_cols)) {
        return(paste0(
          "CorrVars contains columns not in data: ",
          paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")
        ))
      }
    } else if (!value %in% names(data)) {
      return(paste0(mapping, " column is not in data: ", value))
    }
  }

  TRUE
}

arg_to_code <- function(name, value) {
  if (is.logical(value) || is.numeric(value)) {
    return(paste0(name, " = ", deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)))
  }

  paste0(name, " = ", r_string(value))
}

build_autoplots_code <- function(plot_type, input) {
  args <- build_plot_args(plot_type, data = NULL, input = input, include_data = FALSE)
  arg_lines <- c(
    "dt = data",
    unlist(Map(arg_to_code, names(args), args), use.names = FALSE)
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    "p1 <- AutoPlots::", plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(arg_lines, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "p1"
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    all_choices <- column_choices(data)
    if (is.null(selected)) {
      selected <- names(data)
    }

    selected <- selected[selected %in% all_choices]
    if (!length(selected)) {
      selected <- all_choices
    }

    return(selectInput(
      mapping_input_id(mapping),
      mapping,
      choices = all_choices,
      select ... [truncated]
