## 2026-07-04T06:24:12.883Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add basic layout support for saved plots in the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow saved plots p1, p2, p3, etc. to be previewed together using AutoPlots::display_plots_grid().

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement draggable dashboard layout.
- Do not implement export yet.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. In the Layout tab, add controls:
- selectInput("layout_type", "Layout", choices = c("Grid"), selected = "Grid")
- numericInput("layout_cols", "Columns", value = 2, min = 1, max = 4, step = 1)

2. Use saved_plots$plots as the source of plots.

3. Render the layout preview using:

AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
  plots = saved_plots$plots,
  cols = input$layout_cols
)

4. If no saved plots exist, show:
"Add plots from the Plots tab to preview a layout."

5. Add a generated layout code output.

It should generate code like:

plots <- list(p1, p2, p3)

AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
  plots = plots,
  cols = 2
)

6. Add combined generated report code.

The report code should include:
- library(AutoPlots)
- data <- data.table::fread("path/to/data.csv")
- p1 <- ...
- p2 <- ...
- p3 <- ...
- report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(...)
- report

7. Smoke tests:
- Add one plot, Layout tab shows it.
- Add two plots, Layout tab shows both.
- Change columns from 2 to 1, preview updates.
- Generated report code includes p1, p2, and display_plots_grid().
- Saved plots remain unchanged when current plot controls change.

build_autoplots_assignment_code <- function(name, config) {
  args <- build_plot_args_from_config(config, data = NULL, include_data = FALSE)
  arg_lines <- c(
    "dt = data",
    unlist(Map(arg_to_code, names(args), args), use.names = FALSE)
  )

  paste0(
    name, " <- AutoPlots::", config$plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(arg_lines, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_saved_plots_code <- function(saved_code) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    return(paste0(
      "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
      "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
      "list()"
    ))
  }

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n"),
    "\n\n",
    "list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")"
  )
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(
    Plot = name,
    PlotType = config$plot_type,
    Title = option_text("title.text"),
    XVar = mapping_text("XVar"),
    YVar = mapping_text("YVar"),
    GroupVar = mapping_text("GroupVar"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) { ... [truncated]

Added basic grid layout support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Layout tab now has:
  - `Layout` selector with `Grid`
  - `Columns` numeric input
- Saved plots preview through `AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(plots = saved_plots$plots, cols = input$layout_cols)`.
- Empty Layout tab message now says: “Add plots from the Plots tab to preview a layout.”
- Added generated layout code:
  - `plots <- list(p1, p2, ...)`
  - `AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(...)`
- Added generated report code with libraries, data load, saved plot creation, grid report creation, and `report`.

Verified with R smoke tests:
- Layout code includes `p1`, `p2`, `display_plots_grid()`, and selected `cols`.
- Report code includes saved plot assignments and grid report.
- `display_plots_grid()` successfully rendered with one saved plot and with two saved plots.

`app.R` is still untracked, so git still can’t show a normal tracked diff for it.


## 2026-07-04T06:30:57.152Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add section-based layout support for saved plots in the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow saved plots to be organized into named sections and rendered with AutoPlots::display_plots_sections().

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement draggable dashboards.
- Do not implement export yet.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. Update Layout controls.

layout_type should support:
- Grid
- Sections

2. Add section controls.

For now, keep it simple:
- textInput("section_name", "Section Name", value = "Analysis")
- actionButton("assign_section", "Assign All Saved Plots to Section")

3. Maintain section state.

Add reactiveValues:

saved_sections <- reactiveValues(
  sections = list()
)

When Assign All Saved Plots to Section is clicked:
- use input$section_name
- assign all currently saved plots to that section
- store plot names/configs as needed
- do not remove saved plots

4. Render section layout.

If layout_type == "Sections", render:

AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(
  sections = saved_sections$sections,
  cols = input$layout_cols
)

If no sections exist, show:
"Assign saved plots to a section to preview a section layout."

5. Keep Grid behavior unchanged.

If layout_type == "Grid", continue using:

AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
  plots = saved_plots$plots,
  cols = input$layout_cols
)

6. Generated layout code.

If Grid:

plots <- list(p1, p2, p3)

report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
  plots = plots,
  cols = 2
)

If Sections:

sections <- list(
  "Analysis" = list(p1, p2, p3)
)

report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(
  sections = sections,
  cols = 2
)

7. Combined report code should update based on selected layout type.

It should include:
- library(AutoPlots)
- data <- data.table::fread("path/to/data.csv")
- p1 <- ...
- p2 <- ...
- p3 <- ...
- report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(...) OR display_plots_sections(...)
- report

8. Smoke tests:
- Add two plots.
- Grid layout still works.
- Switch to Sections.
- Assign section name "Performance".
- Section preview renders both plots.
- Generated code uses display_plots_sections().
- Switching back to Grid still works.

## 2026-07-04T06:31:05.876Z - assistant

I’ll extend the layout state rather than changing saved plots themselves: saved plots remain the source of truth, and a section is just a named snapshot/list of those saved plot objects. Then the code generators can switch between grid and sections from the same p1/p2 names.

build_layout_code <- function(plot_names, cols = 2) {
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    plot_list <- "list()"
  } else {
    plot_list <- paste0("list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")")
  }

  paste0(
    "plots <- ", plot_list, "\n\n",
    "AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(\n",
    "  plots = plots,\n",
    "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(saved_code, cols = 2) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    "plots <- list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")\n\n",
    "report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(\n",
    "  plots = plots,\n",
    "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "report"
  )
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(
    Plot = name,
    PlotType = config$plot_type,
    Title = option_text("title.text"),
    XVar = mapping_text("XVar"),
    YVar = mapping_text("YVar"),
    GroupVar = mapping_text("GroupVar"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    all_choices <- column_choi ... [truncated]

The app-side code is fine, but the runtime smoke exposed something important: the installed AutoPlots available to this R session does not export `display_plots_sections`, so `AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()` fails before rendering. I’ll still keep the app using the required high-level call, and I’ll add friendly handling so the UI doesn’t crater if the installed package is behind the source.

#' @param title_color CSS color for section titles.
#' @param subtitle_color CSS color for section subtitles.
#' @param font_family CSS font-family.
#' @param save_path Optional file path to save standalone HTML.
#' @param selfcontained Logical passed to htmlwidgets::saveWidget.
#' @param return_code Logical. If TRUE, return list(html = ..., code = ...).
#'
#' @return A browsable sectioned HTML report, or list(html, code) when return_code = TRUE.
#'
#' @export
display_plots_sections <- function(
    sections,
    cols = 2,
    collapsible = TRUE,
    open = TRUE,
    section_titles = NULL,
    section_subtitles = NULL,
    plot_titles = NULL,
    plot_subtitles = NULL,
    gap = "20px",
    section_gap = "22px",
    padding = "0px",
    card = TRUE,
    card_background = NULL,
    card_border = NULL,
    card_shadow = NULL,
    section_background = NULL,
    section_border = NULL,
    section_radius = "16px",
    section_padding = "18px",
    title_color = NULL,
    subtitle_color = NULL,
    font_family = "Segoe UI, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif",
    save_path = NULL,
    selfcontained = TRUE,
    return_code = FALSE
) {

  if (!is.list(sections) || length(sections) == 0L) {
    stop("`sections` must be a non-empty named list of plot lists.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  nsections <- length(sections)

  if (is.null(section_titles)) {
    nms <- names(sections)
    if (!is.null(nms) && all(nzchar(nms))) {
      section_titles <- nms
    } else {
      section_titles <- paste0("Section ", seq_len(nsections))
    }
  }

  section_titles <- .ap_normalize_labels(section_titles, nsections, "section_titles")
  section_subtitles <- .ap_normalize_labels(section_subtitles, nsections, "section_subtitles")

  section_names <- names(sections)
  if (is.null(section_names) || any(!nzchar(section_names))) {
    section_names <- section_titles
  }

  flat_plots <- .ap_flatten_plot_sections(sections)
  inferred_theme <- .ap_infer_theme_from_plots(flat_plots, fallback = "dark")
  layout_theme <- .ap_get_layout_theme_defaults(inferred_theme)

  if (is.null(section_background)) section_background <- layout_theme$section_background
  if (is.null(section_border))     section_border     <- layout_theme$section_border
  if (is.null(card_background))    card_background    <- layout_theme$card_background
  if (is.null(card_border))        card_border        <- layout_theme$card_border
  if (is.null(card_shadow)) ... [truncated]

      style = wrapper_style,
      htmltools::tagList(section_nodes)
    )
  )

  if (!is.null(save_path)) {
    htmlwidgets::saveWidget(
      widget = out,
      file = save_path,
      selfcontained = selfcontained
    )
  }

  code_lines <- c(
    "AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(",
    "  sections = list(",
    paste0(
      "    ", .ap_deparse_arg(section_titles), " = list(...)",
      collapse = ",\n"
    ),
    "  ),",
    paste0("  cols = ", cols, ","),
    paste0("  collapsible = ", if (isTRUE(collapsible)) "TRUE" else "FALSE", ","),
    paste0("  open = ", if (isTRUE(open)) "TRUE" else "FALSE", ","),
    paste0("  card = ", if (isTRUE(card)) "TRUE" else "FALSE"),
    ")"
  )

  generated_code <- paste(code_lines, collapse = "\n")

  if (isTRUE(return_code)) {
    return(list(
      html = out,
      code = generated_code
    ))
  }

  out
}

## 2026-07-04T06:34:13.863Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Added section-based layout support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Layout selector now supports `Grid` and `Sections`.
- Added `Section Name` plus `Assign All Saved Plots to Section`.
- Added `saved_sections <- reactiveValues(sections = list())`.
- Sections store named saved plot lists without removing saved plots.
- Grid preview still uses `AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()`.
- Section preview uses `AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()`.
- Layout/report code generation now switches between grid and sections.
- Combined report code includes plot assignments, layout call, and `report`.

Verified:
- Grid generated code and render smoke passed.
- Sections generated code passed, including:
  - `"Performance" = list(p1, p2)`
  - `AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(...)`
- Full local section render could not be completed because the installed AutoPlots in this R session does not export `display_plots_sections`, while the source tree/NAMESPACE does. `devtools::load_all()` was also blocked by missing `webshot2`.

I added a friendly layout error message so an out-of-date installed package won’t dump a raw Shiny error in the UI.


## 2026-07-04T06:38:59.668Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add HTML export support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to export the current report layout as a self-contained HTML file using AutoPlots::save_widget().

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement PNG export yet.
- Do not implement R-code file export yet.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Use the current selected layout object:
  - Grid -> AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
  - Sections -> AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()

Requirements:

1. In the Export tab, add controls:
- textInput("export_dir", "Export Directory", value = getwd())
- textInput("export_name", "File Name", value = "autoplots_report")
- actionButton("export_html", "Export HTML", class = "btn-primary")

2. Build the current report object from saved plots and selected layout.

Create helper/reactive:

current_report <- reactive({
  if no saved plots, return NULL

  if input$layout_type == "Grid":
    AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
      plots = saved_plots$plots,
      cols = input$layout_cols
    )

  if input$layout_type == "Sections":
    AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(
      sections = saved_sections$sections,
      cols = input$layout_cols
    )
})

3. On Export HTML click:
- validate that current_report() exists
- validate export_dir and export_name
- call:

AutoPlots::save_widget(
  widget = current_report(),
  path = input$export_dir,
  name = input$export_name,
  selfcontained = TRUE,
  overwrite = TRUE,
  open = FALSE
)

4. Show a success message with the output path.

Example:
"Exported HTML to C:/.../autoplots_report.html"

5. Show a friendly error if export fails.

6. Generated code should include the export code.

At the bottom of combined report code, add:

AutoPlots::save_widget(
  widget = report,
  path = "path/to/output",
  name = "autoplots_report",
  selfcontained = TRUE,
  overwrite = TRUE,
  open = FALSE
)

7. Smoke tests:
- Add two plots.
- Preview Grid.
- Export HTML.
- Confirm the HTML file exists.
- Open the HTML file in browser and confirm plots appear.
- Switch to Sections.
- Assign section.
- Export HTML again.
- Confirm section layout exports.
- Try exporting with no saved plots and confirm friendly error.

build_report_code <- function(saved_code, section_plot_names = list(), layout_type = "Grid", cols = 2) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,
    layout_type = layout_type,
    cols = cols
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    layout_code
  )
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(
    Plot = name,
    PlotType = config$plot_type,
    Title = option_text("title.text"),
    XVar = mapping_text("XVar"),
    YVar = mapping_text("YVar"),
    GroupVar = mapping_text("GroupVar"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

layout_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Layout could not be rendered."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    all_choices <- column_choices(data)
    if (is.null(selected)) {
      selected <- names(data)

## 2026-07-04T06:41:03.657Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Added HTML export support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Export tab now has `Export Directory`, `File Name`, and `Export HTML`.
- Added `current_report()` reactive that builds the selected layout:
  - Grid via `AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()`
  - Sections via `AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()`
- Export button validates saved plots, layout, directory, and file name.
- Export calls `AutoPlots::save_widget(..., selfcontained = TRUE, overwrite = TRUE, open = FALSE)`.
- Success/failure messages show in the Export tab.
- Combined report code now appends the matching `AutoPlots::save_widget()` call.

Verified:
- Generated Grid report code includes `display_plots_grid()` and `save_widget()`.
- Generated Sections report code includes `display_plots_sections()` and `save_widget()`.
- Runtime report object creation passed for Grid.

One blocker on the full file-write smoke: the installed `AutoPlots` package in this R session does not currently export `save_widget()`, so the actual HTML export call is expected to show the friendly `Export failed:` message until that installed package/API is updated.


## 2026-07-04T06:41:51.850Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add R code export and Export All support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to export the generated report R code to a .R file, and add an Export All button that saves HTML + R code.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement PNG export yet.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. In the Export tab, add:
- actionButton("export_code", "Export R Code", class = "btn-secondary")
- actionButton("export_all", "Export All", class = "btn-success")

2. Reuse existing export_dir and export_name.

R code output path should be:
file.path(input$export_dir, paste0(input$export_name, ".R"))

HTML output path should remain:
file.path(input$export_dir, paste0(input$export_name, ".html"))

3. Add helper:

write_report_code <- function(code, path, name)

or equivalent.

It should:
- validate directory
- create directory if needed
- write the current combined report code to paste0(name, ".R")
- return normalized file path

4. On Export R Code click:
- validate that combined report code exists
- write code to .R file
- show success message with output path
- show friendly error if it fails

5. On Export All click:
- validate that current_report() exists
- export HTML using AutoPlots::save_widget()
- export R code using the same generated combined report code
- show success message listing both paths

6. Combined generated report code should remain the source of truth.

Do not regenerate a separate version of code for export. Use the same code shown in the app.

7. Smoke tests:
- Add two plots.
- Preview Grid.
- Export R Code.
- Confirm .R file exists and contains p1, p2, report, and save_widget code.
- Export All.
- Confirm both .html and .R files exist.
- Switch to Sections.
- Export All.
- Confirm generated .R code uses display_plots_sections().
- Try Export R Code with no saved plots and show friendly error.

build_export_code <- function(export_dir = "path/to/output", export_name = "autoplots_report") {
  paste0(
    "AutoPlots::save_widget(\n",
    "  widget = report,\n",
    "  path = ", r_string(export_dir), ",\n",
    "  name = ", r_string(export_name), ",\n",
    "  selfcontained = TRUE,\n",
    "  overwrite = TRUE,\n",
    "  open = FALSE\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(
  saved_code,
  section_plot_names = list(),
  layout_type = "Grid",
  cols = 2,
  export_dir = "path/to/output",
  export_name = "autoplots_report"
) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,
    layout_type = layout_type,
    cols = cols
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    layout_code,
    "\n\n",
    build_export_code(export_dir = export_dir, export_name = export_name)
  )
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(
    Plot = name,
    PlotType = config$plot_type,
    Title = option_text("title.text"),
    XVar = mapping_text("XVar"),
    YVar = mapping_text("YVar"),
    GroupVar = mapping_text("GroupVar"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built. ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-04T06:43:38.391Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Added R code export and Export All support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Export tab now has:
  - `Export HTML`
  - `Export R Code`
  - `Export All`
- Added `write_report_code()` to create the export directory if needed and write `<export_name>.R`.
- Added `current_report_code()` so the visible combined report code and exported `.R` file use the same generated string.
- `Export R Code` writes the combined report code and shows the output path.
- `Export All` builds the current report, calls `AutoPlots::save_widget()`, writes the `.R` file, and reports both paths.
- Friendly errors are shown for missing saved plots, missing file name, bad directory creation, or export failures.

Verified:
- `.R` export smoke passed: file was created and contains `p1`, `p2`, `report`, and `AutoPlots::save_widget()`.
- Sections report-code smoke passed and uses `display_plots_sections()`.
- Runtime Grid report object still builds.

Same environment caveat as before: this installed `AutoPlots` does not export `save_widget()`, so HTML and Export All HTML-write smoke are skipped here until that API is available in the installed package.


## 2026-07-04T06:46:27.528Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add saved plot editing support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to select a saved plot, load its configuration back into the plot builder controls, modify it, rebuild it, and update the saved plot.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven UI.
- Do not implement drag/drop layout yet.
- Make small targeted changes.

Requirements:

1. In the saved plot list panel, add:
- selectInput("selected_saved_plot", "Saved Plot", choices = saved plot names)
- actionButton("load_saved_plot", "Load Plot for Editing")
- actionButton("update_saved_plot", "Update Saved Plot")
- actionButton("duplicate_saved_plot", "Duplicate Plot")

2. When saved plots change, update selected_saved_plot choices.

3. Load Plot for Editing:
When clicked:
- get selected plot name, e.g. p1
- retrieve saved_plots$configs[[plot_name]]
- update plot_type
- update mappings
- update option controls
- update title/subtitle/theme/etc.
- do not automatically rebuild plot
- show message: "Loaded p1 for editing. Click Build / Refresh Plot."

4. Update Saved Plot:
When clicked:
- require selected_saved_plot
- require current built plot
- replace saved_plots$plots[[plot_name]]
- replace saved_plots$configs[[plot_name]]
- replace saved_plots$code[[plot_name]]
- keep the same plot name
- update generated all-plots/report code
- show message: "Updated p1"

5. Duplicate Plot:
When clicked:
- require selected_saved_plot
- copy its plot object/config/code
- assign next plot name, e.g. p4
- add to saved plot list
- show message: "Duplicated p1 as p4"

6. Ensure saved plots are immutable unless Update Saved Plot is clicked.
Changing current controls should not alter saved plots.

7. Preserve mapping persistence behavior.
Loading a saved plot should not break dynamic mapping UI.

8. Smoke tests:
- Add Line as p1.
- Add Bar as p2.
- Select p1 and Load Plot for Editing.
- Change title.text.
- Build / Refresh Plot.
- Click Update Saved Plot.
- Layout preview reflects updated p1.
- Select p2 and Duplicate Plot.
- New p3 appears.
- Generated report code includes p1, p2, p3.

I’ll make saved plot editing explicit and opt-in: loading a saved config will populate the builder and clear the current built result, so the saved plot only changes after the user rebuilds and clicks Update. That keeps the immutability rule intact.

}

logical_value <- function(value) {
  isTRUE(value)
}

numeric_or_null <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || is.na(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  as.numeric(value)
}

plot_spec <- function(plot_type) {
  plot_registry[[plot_type]]
}

mapping_input_id <- function(mapping) {
  paste0("mapping_", mapping)
}

mapping_value <- function(input, mapping) {
  value <- input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]]
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    value <- selected_vector(value)
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    return(value)
  }

  selected_value(value)
}

active_mappings <- function(plot_type) {
  spec <- plot_spec(plot_type)
  c(spec$mappings, spec$optional_mappings)
}

option_control <- function(option_name) {
  opt <- option_registry[[option_name]]
  if (is.null(opt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  switch(
    opt$type,
    select = selectInput(opt$input_id, opt$label, choices = opt$choices, selected = opt$default),
    text = textInput(opt$input_id, opt$label, value = opt$default),
    checkbox = checkboxInput(opt$input_id, opt$label, value = opt$default),
    numeric = numericInput(opt$input_id, opt$label, value = opt$default),
    NULL
  )
}

option_value <- function(input, option_name) {
  opt <- option_registry[[option_name]]
  if (is.null(opt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value <- input[[opt$input_id]]
  switch(
    opt$type,
    checkbox = logical_value(value),
    numeric = numeric_or_null(value),
    selected_value(value)
  )
}

add_option_arg <- function(args, option_name, value) {
  if (identical(option_name, "AutoAggregate")) {
    args$PreAgg <- !isTRUE(value)
    return(args)
  }

  if (identical(option_name, "AggMethod")) {
    args$AggMethod <- value
    return(args)
  }

  args[[option_name]] <- value
  args
}

build_plot_args <- function(
  plot_type,
  data,
  input,
  include_data = TRUE
) {
  args <- list()
  if (include_data) {
    args$dt <- data
  }

  for (mapping in active_mappings(plot_type)) {
    args[[mapping]] <- mapping_value(input, mapping)
  }

  for (option_name in plot_spec(plot_type)$options) {
    args <- add_option_arg(args, option_name, option_value(input, option_name))
  }

  args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
}

build_autoplots_call <- function(plot_type, data, input) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", plot_type)
  args <- build_plot_args(plot_type, data, input)
  do.call(plot_fun, args) ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-04T06:49:36.790Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Added saved plot editing support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Saved Plots panel now has:
  - saved plot selector
  - Load Plot for Editing
  - Update Saved Plot
  - Duplicate Plot
- Saved plot selector choices update as plots are added/removed/duplicated.
- Loading a saved plot restores `PlotType`, mappings, and registry-driven options back into the builder.
- Loading clears the current built plot so saved plots stay immutable until the user rebuilds and clicks Update.
- Updating replaces the selected saved plot object/config/code under the same name.
- Duplicating creates the next plot name, e.g. `p3`, and regenerates code with the new assignment name.
- If an updated plot is already in a section, that section reference is refreshed too.

Verified:
- Helper smoke passed for p-name generation and p1/p2/p3 report code.
- `shiny::testServer()` smoke passed:
  - added p1 Line
  - added p2 Bar
  - loaded p1 for editing and cleared current build
  - updated p1 with changed `title.text`
  - duplicated p2 as p3
  - generated code includes p1, p2, p3 correctly.


## 2026-07-04T06:53:39.792Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add plot ordering and per-plot section assignment to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow saved plots to be assigned individually to named sections and ordered within the report.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Do not implement drag/drop yet.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. Add saved plot metadata.

Each saved plot should have metadata including:
- plot_name
- plot_type
- section_name
- sort_order

If metadata already exists in configs, extend it carefully.

Default behavior:
- new plots get section_name = "Analysis"
- new plots get sort_order = next integer

2. Add section assignment controls.

In the saved plot panel, add:
- selectInput("section_for_plot", "Section", choices = existing sections plus typed/new section support if simple)
- textInput("new_section_name", "New Section", value = "")
- actionButton("assign_plot_section", "Assign Plot to Section")

Simple acceptable implementation:
- user selects a saved plot
- enters section name
- clicks Assign Plot to Section
- selected plot metadata section_name updates

3. Add ordering controls.

Add:
- actionButton("move_plot_up", "Move Up")
- actionButton("move_plot_down", "Move Down")

Behavior:
- selected saved plot swaps sort_order with previous/next plot
- ordering should affect grid preview, section preview, and generated code

4. Update saved plot list.

Show:
- plot name
- plot type
- section
- order
- title.text
- XVar
- YVar
- GroupVar if available

5. Update Grid layout.

Grid layout should render saved plots ordered by sort_order.

6. Update Sections layout.

Sections layout should be built from saved plot metadata:

sections <- list(
  "Performance" = list(p1, p2),
  "Distribution" = list(p3, p4)
)

Within each section, plots should be ordered by sort_order.

Section order can be first appearance by sort_order.

7. Update generated report code.

The generated code should reflect plot ordering and section assignment.

For Grid:

plots <- list(p1, p2, p3)

report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
  plots = plots,
  cols = 2
)

For Sections:

sections <- list(
  "Performance" = list(p1, p2),
  "Distribution" = list(p3, p4)
)

report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(
  sections = sections,
  cols = 2
)

8. Preserve existing editing/duplication behavior.

- Updating a plot should preserve section_name and sort_order.
- Duplicating a plot should copy section_name but receive a new sort_order at the end.
- Removing a plot should clean up metadata.

9. Smoke tests:
- Add Line p1, Bar p2, HeatMap p3.
- Assign p1 and p2 to "Performance".
- Assign p3 to "Diagnostics".
- Switch to Sections layout.
- Confirm section preview renders correctly.
- Move p3 up/down and confirm order updates.
- Generated code uses display_plots_sections() with correct section lists.
- Switch to Grid and confirm ordered plot list renders.

build_autoplots_assignment_code <- function(name, config) {
  args <- build_plot_args_from_config(config, data = NULL, include_data = FALSE)
  arg_lines <- c(
    "dt = data",
    unlist(Map(arg_to_code, names(args), args), use.names = FALSE)
  )

  paste0(
    name, " <- AutoPlots::", config$plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(arg_lines, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")"
  )
}

next_plot_name <- function(plot_names) {
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    return("p1")
  }

  plot_ids <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(sub("^p", "", plot_names)))
  plot_ids <- plot_ids[!is.na(plot_ids)]
  if (!length(plot_ids)) {
    return("p1")
  }

  paste0("p", max(plot_ids) + 1L)
}

build_saved_plots_code <- function(saved_code) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    return(paste0(
      "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
      "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
      "list()"
    ))
  }

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n"),
    "\n\n",
    "list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")"
  )
}

plot_list_code <- function(plot_names) {
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    return("list()")
  }

  paste0("list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")")
}

section_list_code <- function(section_plot_names) {
  section_names <- names(section_plot_names)
  if (!length(section_names)) {
    return("list()")
  }

  section_lines <- unlist(lapply(section_names, function(section_name) {
    paste0(
      "  ", r_string(section_name), " = ",
      plot_list_code(section_plot_names[[section_name]])
    )
  }), use.names = FALSE)

  paste0(
    "list(\n",
    paste(section_lines, collapse = ",\n"),
    "\n)"
  )
}

build_layout_code <- function(plot_names, section_plot_names = list(), layout_type = "Grid", cols = 2) {
  if (identical(layout_type, "Sections")) {
    return(paste0(
      "sections <- ", section_list_code(section_plot_names), "\n\n",
      "report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(\n",
      "  sections = sections,\n",
      "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
      ")\n\n",
      "report"
    ))
  }

  paste0(
    "plots <- ", plot_list_code(plot_na ... [truncated]

Added plot ordering and per-plot section assignment in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

- Added saved plot metadata: `plot_name`, `plot_type`, `section_name`, `sort_order`.
- New plots default to section `Analysis` and get the next sort order.
- Saved plot panel now includes:
  - Section selector
  - New Section text input
  - Assign Plot to Section
  - Move Up / Move Down
- Grid layout now renders saved plots ordered by `sort_order`.
- Sections layout is now built from metadata, ordered by `sort_order`.
- Saved plot table now shows section and order.
- Generated report code now reflects ordered plot lists and section assignments.
- Update preserves section/order.
- Duplicate copies section and gets a new order at the end.
- Remove cleans metadata.

Verified:
- Helper smoke passed for ordered Grid and Sections code.
- `shiny::testServer()` smoke passed:
  - added p1 Line, p2 Bar, p3 HeatMap
  - assigned p1/p2 to `Performance`
  - assigned p3 to `Diagnostics`
  - moved p3 up/down and confirmed order changed
  - duplicated p2 with copied section and new order
  - generated code reflects the correct Grid and Sections structures.

`app.R` is still untracked in git, so no tracked diff is available.


## 2026-07-04T07:23:18.197Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add saved project state support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to save the current app/report-builder state to a project .rds file and load it later.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement a database.
- Do not implement cloud sync.
- Do not implement user accounts.
- Keep this local-first.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Make small targeted changes.

Project file format:
Use a single .rds file containing a named list.

Required saved state:
- app_version
- saved_at
- data_path if available
- data_name if available
- plot_configs
- plot_code
- plot_metadata
- layout_type
- layout_cols
- export_dir
- export_name

Optional saved state if already easy:
- current plot type
- current mapping selections
- current option values
- section names
- selected theme

Do not attempt to serialize htmlwidget plot objects as the primary saved artifact.

Important:
Saved project state should store plot configurations and regenerate plot objects on load.

Reason:
htmlwidget objects can be large, brittle, and may not deserialize cleanly across package/app changes.

Requirements:

1. Add Project controls.

In a Project tab or sidebar section, add:
- textInput("project_path", "Project File", value = file.path(getwd(), "autoplots_project.rds"))
- actionButton("save_project", "Save Project", class = "btn-primary")
- actionButton("load_project", "Load Project", class = "btn-secondary")

2. Save Project.

On save_project:
- collect the current project state into a named list
- include app_version = "0.1.0" or existing app version if defined
- include saved_at = Sys.time()
- include saved plot configs/code/metadata
- include layout/export settings
- saveRDS(project_state, input$project_path)
- show success message with normalized path

3. Load Project.

On load_project:
- readRDS(input$project_path)
- validate it is a list
- validate it contains expected fields
- restore saved plot configs/code/metadata
- restore layout/export settings
- restore project/data path info if available
- if data_path exists and file.exists(data_path), reload data automatically
- regenerate saved plot objects from saved configs using the current AutoPlots functions
- if data_path is missing or does not exist, load configs/metadata but show message:
  "Project loaded, but source data file was not found. Re-upload the data to rebuild plots."

4. Regenerating plots.

Create or reuse a helper that can rebuild a plot object from a saved config.

The helper should:
- take data and a saved config
- call the correct AutoPlots high-level function
- return the htmlwidget plot object

Do not eval arbitrary code from the project file.

Use stored config values, not stored generated code, to rebuild plots.

5. Generated code.

Generated report code should still be regenerated from restored configs/metadata after load.

6. Version handling.

If app_version in project file is missing or different:
- do not fail immediately
- show a warning like:
  "Project file version differs from current app version. Attempting to load."

7. Friendly errors.

Handle:
- missing project_path
- file does not exist on load
- invalid .rds structure
- missing source data
- failed plot regeneration

8. Smoke tests:
- Upload CSV.
- Build Line p1.
- Build Bar p2.
- Assign sections.
- Set layout to Sections.
- Save project.
- Restart app.
- Load project.
- Confirm data reloads if data_path exists.
- Confirm p1 and p2 regenerate.
- Confirm sections/layout restore.
- Confirm generated report code matches the restored project.
- Rename or move the CSV.
- Load project again.
- Confirm project metadata loads but app asks user to re-upload data.

## 2026-07-04T07:24:13.370Z - assistant

I’m wiring the server state now. The important design choice: project files store configs/metadata/code, and plots are regenerated from configs when data is available; widget objects are not saved.

The snapshot/restore helpers are in. I’m updating data handling so a loaded project can use a reloaded CSV path, and if the CSV is missing the configs still load while the user gets a clear “re-upload” message.

I’m patching the display-side checks next, because the app currently assumes a Shiny file upload exists. Loaded-project data should count as available data too.

Now I’m adding the actual Save Project / Load Project observers. Load validates the `.rds`, restores configs/code/metadata/settings, then rebuilds plot widgets only if the saved data path is still available.

## 2026-07-04T07:26:52.773Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Added saved project state support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Added a `Project` tab with:
  - project `.rds` path
  - Save Project
  - Load Project
  - status/warning messages
- Project files save a named list with:
  - `app_version`, `saved_at`
  - `data_path`, `data_name`
  - saved plot configs/code/metadata
  - layout/export settings
  - current plot type, mappings, options, section names, selected theme
- Project loading validates structure and warns on version mismatch.
- Plot objects are not serialized; they are regenerated from saved configs when source data exists.
- If source data is missing, configs/metadata still load and the app asks the user to re-upload data.
- Re-uploading data after a project load rebuilds saved plots from configs.
- Generated report code remains regenerated from restored configs/metadata.

Verified:
- Project helper smoke passed: save/read `.rds`, validate structure, detect invalid structure.
- Server smoke passed:
  - load project with existing CSV path
  - regenerate p1/p2 plot objects
  - restore section metadata and report code
  - load project with missing CSV path
  - keep configs/metadata while showing the missing-data warning.

`app.R` remains untracked, so git still cannot show a tracked diff for it.


## 2026-07-04T07:28:56.586Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add project validation and repair support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Make loaded project files robust when source data, columns, saved plots, sections, or metadata are missing/stale.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not rewrite save/load from scratch.
- Do not eval generated code from project files.
- Continue saving configs, not htmlwidget objects.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. Add a project validation helper.

Create a helper like:

validate_project_state <- function(project_state, data = NULL)

It should return a structured list:

list(
  valid = TRUE/FALSE,
  errors = character(),
  warnings = character(),
  repaired_state = project_state
)

2. Validate top-level project structure.

Required fields:
- app_version
- saved_at
- plot_configs
- plot_code
- plot_metadata
- layout_type
- layout_cols
- export_dir
- export_name

If optional fields are missing, warn but do not fail.

3. Validate saved plot consistency.

Check that:
- plot_configs is a named list
- plot_metadata exists for each saved plot
- plot_code exists for each saved plot, if code is still stored
- plot names are valid, e.g. p1, p2, p3
- metadata plot names match config names

Repair where safe:
- remove metadata for plots that no longer exist
- add default metadata for plots missing metadata
- normalize sort_order
- default missing section_name to "Analysis"

4. Validate layout state.

Allowed layout_type values:
- Grid
- Sections

If missing or invalid:
- set layout_type = "Grid"
- add warning

layout_cols:
- coerce to integer
- clamp between 1 and 4
- default to 2 if invalid

5. Validate sections.

If section state exists:
- remove section references to plots that no longer exist
- drop empty sections
- rebuild sections from plot_metadata when possible

Important:
section layout should ultimately be derived from plot_metadata, not from a stale saved_sections object.

6. Validate data compatibility when data is available.

For each saved plot config:
- check required mapping columns exist in current data
- check XVar/YVar/ZVar/GroupVar/etc. columns exist if non-empty
- warn for missing optional columns
- error or mark plot as not rebuildable if required columns are missing

Do not crash the whole project because one plot cannot rebuild.

7. Rebuild plots defensively.

When loading a project:
- validate project state first
- restore state
- if data is available, attempt to rebuild each plot
- if a plot fails, keep its config and metadata, but mark it as failed/rebuild_needed
- show which plots failed and why

8. Add UI status.

In the Project tab or saved plot table, show project load status:
- project loaded successfully
- warnings
- plots rebuilt successfully
- plots needing attention

For saved plots, show a status column:
- Ready
- Needs data
- Missing columns
- Rebuild failed

9. Generated code behavior.

Generated report code should include only plots that are successfully rebuildable/ready.

If some plots are skipped, show a warning in the app.

10. Smoke tests:
- Save a project with p1 and p2.
- Load it normally. Both rebuild.
- Rename one source data column and load. Project loads, but affected plot is marked Missing columns.
- Delete/move source CSV and load. Configs/metadata load, plots marked Needs data.
- Manually corrupt layout_type in the .rds and load. App repairs to Grid and warns.
- Remove metadata for one plot in the .rds and load. App adds default metadata and warns.

normalize_project_path <- function(path) {
  path <- selected_value(path)
  if (is.null(path)) {
    stop("Project file path is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!grepl("\\.rds$", path, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    path <- paste0(path, ".rds")
  }

  path
}

validate_project_state <- function(project_state) {
  if (!is.list(project_state)) {
    stop("Project file is not a valid project list.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  required_fields <- c(
    "plot_configs", "plot_code", "plot_metadata",
    "layout_type", "layout_cols", "export_dir", "export_name"
  )
  missing_fields <- setdiff(required_fields, names(project_state))
  if (length(missing_fields)) {
    stop(
      "Project file is missing required fields: ",
      paste(missing_fields, collapse = ", "),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  TRUE
}

save_project_state <- function(project_state, path) {
  project_path <- normalize_project_path(path)
  project_dir <- dirname(project_path)

  if (!dir.exists(project_dir)) {
    dir.create(project_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(project_dir)) {
    stop("Project directory could not be created.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  saveRDS(project_state, project_path)
  normalizePath(project_path, winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config, metadata = NULL) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(

## 2026-07-04T07:30:22.123Z - assistant

Ready plots now drive layouts/report code. I’m patching add/update/duplicate/remove and rebuild so status is set consistently and failed plots keep their configs instead of disappearing from the table.


## 2026-07-04T07:30:32.439Z - assistant

Rebuild now marks plot statuses. I’m adding status updates around normal user actions, and making “Needs data” the default for loaded configs before data is available.


## 2026-07-04T07:30:49.942Z - assistant

I’m updating load to consume the structured validation result now: repair first, restore repaired configs/metadata/code, then mark all plots Needs data unless rebuild proves them Ready.


## 2026-07-04T07:30:56.245Z - assistant

Version warnings now come from validation. I’m patching the load observer to use repaired state and to set per-plot statuses cleanly.

  observeEvent(input$update_saved_plot, {
    plot_name <- selected_value(input$selected_saved_plot)
    if (is.null(plot_name) || !plot_name %in% names(saved_plots$plots)) {
      plot_list_message("Select a saved plot to update.")
      return()
    }

    if (is.null(plot_result()) || is.null(plot_config()) || !is.null(plot_error())) {
      plot_list_message("Build the edited plot successfully before updating the saved plot.")
      return()
    }

    config <- plot_config()
    saved_plots$plots[[plot_name]] <- plot_result()
    saved_plots$configs[[plot_name]] <- config
    saved_plots$code[[plot_name]] <- build_autoplots_assignment_code(plot_name, config)
    metadata <- saved_plots$metadata[[plot_name]]
    if (is.null(metadata)) {
      metadata <- plot_metadata(
        plot_name = plot_name,
        config = config,
        section_name = "Analysis",
        sort_order = next_sort_order(saved_plots$metadata)
      )
    }
    metadata$plot_type <- config$plot_type
    saved_plots$metadata[[plot_name]] <- metadata
    saved_plots$status[[plot_name]] <- list(status = "Ready", message = "")
    update_saved_plot_references(plot_name, plot_result())
    plot_list_message(paste("Updated", plot_name))
  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)

  observeEvent(input$duplicate_saved_plot, {
    plot_name <- selected_value(input$selected_saved_plot)
    if (is.null(plot_name) || !plot_name %in% names(saved_plots$plots)) {
      plot_list_message("Select a saved plot to duplicate.")
      return()
    }

    new_plot_name <- next_plot_name(names(saved_plots$plots))
    config <- saved_plots$configs[[plot_name]]
    metadata <- saved_plots$metadata[[plot_name]]
    section_name <- if (is.null(metadata$section_name)) "Analysis" else metadata$section_name
    saved_plots$plots[[new_plot_name]] <- saved_plots$plots[[plot_name]]
    saved_plots$configs[[new_plot_name]] <- config
    saved_plots$code[[new_plot_name]] <- build_autoplots_assignment_code(new_plot_name, config)
    saved_plots$metadata[[new_plot_name]] <- plot_metadata(
      plot_name = new_plot_name,
      config = config,
      section_name = section_name,
      sort_order = next_sort_order(saved_plots$metadata)
    )

## 2026-07-04T07:33:57.909Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Added project validation and repair support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- `validate_project_state(project_state, data = NULL)` now returns structured validation:
  - `valid`
  - `errors`
  - `warnings`
  - `repaired_state`
- It repairs safe stale project state:
  - missing/default metadata
  - stale metadata for deleted plots
  - mismatched metadata plot names
  - missing sections
  - invalid `layout_type`
  - invalid/clamped `layout_cols`
  - missing plot code
- Added data compatibility checks for saved plot mappings.
- Added per-plot statuses:
  - `Ready`
  - `Needs data`
  - `Missing columns`
  - `Rebuild failed`
- Saved plot table now shows `Status`.
- Project loading now:
  - validates and repairs first
  - restores configs/code/metadata
  - rebuilds plots defensively if data exists
  - keeps configs/metadata even when plots cannot rebuild
  - reports warnings and failed plots
- Report/layout/generated code now includes only `Ready` plots.

Verified:
- Validation helper smoke passed for normal, missing metadata, corrupt layout, missing version, and missing columns.
- Server smoke passed for:
  - normal load/rebuild
  - renamed source column -> `Missing columns`
  - missing source CSV -> `Needs data`
  - corrupt `layout_type` -> repaired to `Grid`
  - missing metadata -> default metadata added.


## 2026-07-04T07:37:07.613Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Add portable project bundle support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to save and load a self-contained local project folder containing the project state and a copy of the source data.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement cloud sync.
- Do not implement database storage.
- Do not implement zip export yet.
- Do not eval generated code from project files.
- Continue saving configs, not htmlwidget plot objects.
- Keep this local-first.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Bundle format:
A project bundle is a folder:

<bundle_dir>/
  project.rds
  data.csv
  exports/

Requirements:

1. Add Project tab controls:
- textInput("bundle_dir", "Project Bundle Directory", value = file.path(getwd(), "autoplots_project"))
- actionButton("save_bundle", "Save Project Bundle", class = "btn-primary")
- actionButton("load_bundle", "Load Project Bundle", class = "btn-secondary")

2. Save Project Bundle.

On save_bundle:
- validate bundle_dir
- create bundle_dir if needed
- create file.path(bundle_dir, "exports") if needed
- save project state to file.path(bundle_dir, "project.rds")
- if uploaded/source data is available:
  - copy source CSV into file.path(bundle_dir, "data.csv")
  - update project state data_path to the bundled data.csv path
  - optionally store original_data_path separately
- saveRDS the updated bundled project state
- show success message with bundle path

3. Load Project Bundle.

On load_bundle:
- look for file.path(bundle_dir, "project.rds")
- load it using existing project validation/repair
- if file.path(bundle_dir, "data.csv") exists, prefer that data file over any absolute data_path stored inside project.rds
- reload data from bundled data.csv
- regenerate saved plots from configs
- restore layout/export settings
- set export_dir to file.path(bundle_dir, "exports") unless project state explicitly overrides it safely
- show success/warnings

4. Do not break existing Save Project / Load Project .rds behavior.

Keep both workflows:
- Save Project File
- Load Project File
- Save Project Bundle
- Load Project Bundle

5. Generated code behavior.

When loaded from a bundle, generated code should use the bundled data path, not the old absolute path.

Example:

data <- data.table::fread("C:/.../autoplots_project/data.csv")

6. Add validation.

Friendly errors for:
- missing bundle_dir
- missing project.rds
- missing bundled data.csv
- invalid project.rds
- failed data reload
- failed plot rebuild

7. Smoke tests:
- Upload CSV.
- Build p1 and p2.
- Assign sections.
- Save Project Bundle.
- Confirm folder contains project.rds, data.csv, exports/.
- Restart app.
- Load Project Bundle.
- Confirm data reloads from bundle data.csv.
- Confirm p1 and p2 rebuild.
- Confirm layout/sections restore.
- Confirm export_dir points to bundle exports/.
- Move original CSV elsewhere.
- Load bundle again.
- Confirm it still works.

build_layout_code <- function(plot_names, section_plot_names = list(), layout_type = "Grid", cols = 2) {
  if (identical(layout_type, "Sections")) {
    return(paste0(
      "sections <- ", section_list_code(section_plot_names), "\n\n",
      "report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(\n",
      "  sections = sections,\n",
      "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
      ")\n\n",
      "report"
    ))
  }

  paste0(
    "plots <- ", plot_list_code(plot_names), "\n\n",
    "report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(\n",
    "  plots = plots,\n",
    "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "report"
  )
}

build_export_code <- function(export_dir = "path/to/output", export_name = "autoplots_report") {
  paste0(
    "AutoPlots::save_widget(\n",
    "  widget = report,\n",
    "  path = ", r_string(export_dir), ",\n",
    "  name = ", r_string(export_name), ",\n",
    "  selfcontained = TRUE,\n",
    "  overwrite = TRUE,\n",
    "  open = FALSE\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(
  saved_code,
  section_plot_names = list(),
  layout_type = "Grid",
  cols = 2,
  export_dir = "path/to/output",
  export_name = "autoplots_report"
) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,
    layout_type = layout_type,
    cols = cols
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    layout_code,
    "\n\n",
    build_export_code(export_dir = export_dir, export_name = export_name)
  )
}

write_report_code <- function(code, path, name) {
  export_dir <- selected_value(path)
  if (is.null(export_dir)) {
    stop("Export directory is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  export_name <- selected_value(name)
  if (is.null(export_name)) {
    stop("File name is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(export_dir)) {
    dir.create(export_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(export_dir)) {
    stop("Export directory could not be created.", call. = FALSE)
  }

Output:
    result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, "plot_metadata was missing or invalid and default metadata was added.")
  }

  stale_metadata <- setdiff(names(repaired$plot_metadata), plot_names)
  if (length(stale_metadata)) {
    repaired$plot_metadata[stale_metadata] <- NULL
    result$warnings <- c(
      result$warnings,
      paste("Removed stale metadata for missing plots:", paste(stale_metadata, collapse = ", "))
    )
  }

  for (plot_name in plot_names) {
    config <- repaired$plot_configs[[plot_name]]

    if (is.null(repaired$plot_code[[plot_name]])) {
      repaired$plot_code[[plot_name]] <- build_autoplots_assignment_code(plot_name, config)
      result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, paste("Rebuilt missing code for", plot_name))
    }

    metadata <- repaired$plot_metadata[[plot_name]]
    if (is.null(metadata) || !is.list(metadata)) {
      metadata <- plot_metadata(
        plot_name = plot_name,
        config = config,
        section_name = "Analysis",
        sort_order = NA_integer_
      )
      result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, paste("Added default metadata for", plot_name))
    }

    if (!identical(metadata$plot_name, plot_name)) {
      metadata$plot_name <- plot_name
      result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, paste("Repaired metadata plot name for", plot_name))
    }
    metadata$plot_type <- config$plot_type
    if (is.null(metadata$section_name) || !nzchar(metadata$section_name)) {
      metadata$section_name <- "Analysis"
      result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, paste("Defaulted section to Analysis for", plot_name))
    }
    metadata$sort_order <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(metadata$sort_order))
    repaired$plot_metadata[[plot_name]] <- metadata
  }

  ordered_names <- ordered_plot_names_from_metadata(repaired$plot_metadata)
  for (index in seq_along(ordered_names)) {
    repaired$plot_metadata[[ordered_names[index]]]$sort_order <- index
  }

  if (is.null(repaired$layout_type) || !repaired$layout_type %in% c("Grid", "Sections")) {
    repaired$layout_type <- "Grid"
    result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, "Invalid layout_type repaired to Grid.")
  }

  layout_cols <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(repaired$layout_cols))
  if (is.na(layout_cols)) {
    layout_cols <- 2L
    result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, "Invalid layout_cols repaired to 2.")
  }
  repaired$layout_cols <- max(1L, min(4L, layout_cols))

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  mapping_state <- reactiveValues(values = list())
  saved_plots <- reactiveValues(
    plots = list(),
    configs = list(),
    code = list(),
    metadata = list(),
    status = list()
  )
  saved_sections <- reactiveValues(
    sections = list()
  )
  plot_result <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_error <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_config <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_list_message <- reactiveVal("")
  export_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_data <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  project_data_info <- reactiveVal(list(path = NULL, name = NULL))

  layout_cols_value <- function() {
    cols <- input$layout_cols
    if (is.null(cols) || is.na(cols)) {
      return(2)
    }

    as.integer(cols)
  }

  export_name_value <- function() {
    name <- selected_value(input$export_name)
    if (is.null(name)) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(name))
  }

  export_output_path <- function(export_dir, export_name) {
    file.path(export_dir, paste0(export_name, ".html"))
  }

  current_report_code <- function() {
    ready_names <- ready_plot_names()
    if (!length(ready_names)) {
      stop("No ready saved plots are available. Rebuild or repair saved plots before exporting R code.", call. = FALSE)
    }

    build_report_code(
      saved_code = ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$code, ready_names),
      section_plot_names = section_plot_names(ready_only = TRUE),
      layout_type = input$layout_type,
      cols = layout_cols_value(),
      export_dir = default_value(selected_value(input$export_dir), "path/to/output"),
      export_name = default_value(export_name_value(), "autoplots_report")
    )
  }

  ordered_plot_names <- function() {
    ordered_plot_names_from_metadata(saved_plots$metadata)
  }

  ready_plot_names <- function() {
    plot_names <- ordered_plot_names()
    plot_names[vapply(plot_names, function(plot_name) {
      identical(saved_plots$status[[plot_name]]$status, "Ready")
    }, logical(1))]
  }

  ordered_saved_plots <- function() {
    ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$plots, ready_plot_names())
  }

  section_plot_names <- function(ready_only = FALSE) {
    metadata <- saved_plots$metadata
    if (isTRUE(ready_only)) {
      metadata <- ordered_list_by_names(metadata, ready_plot_names())
    }

    section_plot_names_from_metadata(metadata)
  }

  section_plot_objects <- function ... [truncated]

## 2026-07-04T07:40:05.314Z - assistant

Topics: autoplots


Added portable project bundle support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Project tab now includes:
  - `Project Bundle Directory`
  - `Save Project Bundle`
  - `Load Project Bundle`
- Bundle format is:
  - `project.rds`
  - `data.csv`
  - `exports/`
- Save Bundle:
  - creates the bundle folder and `exports/`
  - copies or writes the current data to `data.csv`
  - saves project state to `project.rds`
  - updates project state to use bundled `data.csv`
  - points `export_dir` at bundle `exports/`
- Load Bundle:
  - validates `project.rds`
  - requires bundled `data.csv`
  - prefers bundled `data.csv` over old absolute paths
  - regenerates plots from configs
  - restores layout/sections/export settings
- Generated report code now uses the current/restored data path, so bundle-loaded projects generate `data.table::fread(".../autoplots_project/data.csv")`.

Verified:
- Helper smoke confirmed bundle folder structure and generated code uses bundled `data.csv`.
- Server smoke passed:
  - saved bundle with `project.rds`, `data.csv`, `exports/`
  - deleted original CSV
  - loaded bundle successfully from copied `data.csv`
  - rebuilt p1/p2
  - restored section layout
  - set export dir to bundle `exports/`
  - generated report code uses bundled data path.

## 2026-07-04T15:38:39.047Z - user

Topics: genai


I was chatting with pro chatgpt about this project and referenced the previous headaches I ran into with Quantico, which was designed to be a similar app - pretty all-encompassing, advanced, and user friendly but the code base became a night and so did edge-case management. Create a service contract design document for the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Prevent Quantico-style edge-case sprawl by defining a standard service/module interface.

Rules:
- Do not modify app behavior.
- Do not refactor existing code yet.
- Create documentation only.
- Focus on future architecture.

Create:
docs/service_contract.md

Include:

1. Problem statement
- Quantico became difficult to maintain because each service/module managed edge cases differently.
- The new app should use a standard service contract.

2. Core principle
Every module should:
- validate inputs
- execute bounded logic
- return a standard result object
- never leak raw errors directly into the app UI

3. Standard result object

Define fields:
- status: success, warning, error, needs_input
- value
- artifacts
- messages
- warnings
- errors
- diagnostics
- code
- metadata

4. Error taxonomy

Include common error codes:
- DATA_MISSING
- COLUMN_MISSING
- COLUMN_TYPE_INVALID
- CONFIG_INVALID
- OPTION_INVALID
- PLOT_NOT_SUPPORTED
- EXPORT_PATH_INVALID
- PROJECT_VERSION_MISMATCH
- PROJECT_DATA_MISSING
- GENAI_JSON_INVALID
- GENAI_SCHEMA_INVALID
- RUNTIME_ERROR

5. Service examples

Define expected contracts for:
- plot_service
- project_service
- export_service
- genai_service
- eda_service
- modeling_service
- forecasting_service

6. Shiny integration rule

The UI should not contain deep business logic.
The UI should:
- collect inputs
- call services
- display standard result messages
- render returned artifacts

7. Module boundary rule

Analytics modules should return report artifacts, not mutate app state directly.

8. Future refactor path

Recommend introducing:
R/services/service_result.R
R/services/validators.R
R/services/plot_service.R
R/services/project_service.R
R/services/export_service.R
R/services/genai_service.R

## 2026-07-04T15:43:37.459Z - user

Topics: autoplots


## Electron wrapper repository

This app may be run inside an Electron shell maintained outside the Analytics Shiny App repository.

Known Electron wrapper source:
- AdrianAntico/<ELECTRON_FORK_REPO_NAME>

The Analytics Shiny App repository owns:
- Shiny app logic
- AutoPlots calls
- project state
- export behavior
- generated report code
- UI behavior inside the Shiny app

The Electron wrapper repository owns:
- Electron startup
- R/Shiny process launch
- local server lifecycle
- app window behavior
- packaged desktop behavior
- installer/build configuration if present
- filesystem permissions or path bridging specific to Electron

When an Electron smoke test fails, first determine whether the failure belongs to the Shiny app repo or the Electron wrapper repo.

Likely Shiny app repo issues:
- plot build fails in browser and Electron
- project save/load fails in browser and Electron
- export function fails in browser and Electron
- generated code is wrong
- UI observer/reactive logic fails

Likely Electron wrapper repo issues:
- Electron app does not launch
- R/Shiny backend does not start
- browser version works but Electron version does not
- file paths behave differently only in Electron
- export permissions differ only in Electron
- app closes but R process remains running
- packaged app cannot find R/packages/app files
- environment variables/API keys are not available in Electron

If the issue appears Electron-specific, inspect and modify the Electron wrapper repository, not the Analytics Shiny App repo.

Got it. I’ll treat Electron as a separate wrapper boundary going forward:

- Analytics Shiny App owns Shiny behavior, AutoPlots calls, project state, exports, generated code, and in-app UI logic.
- Electron wrapper owns process launch, local server lifecycle, window behavior, packaging/installers, Electron-only file/path behavior, and desktop runtime concerns.

For future Electron smoke failures, I’ll first classify whether the same issue reproduces in the normal browser Shiny app. If it’s browser + Electron, I’ll work in the Shiny app repo. If it’s Electron-only, I’ll inspect the Electron wrapper repo instead.

## 2026-07-04T15:46:13.763Z - user

Topics: genai, autoplots


Refactor export logic into a standard service-result pattern.

Goal:
Start applying the service contract architecture by moving HTML/R-code/Export All logic into a bounded export service.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not change user-facing behavior.
- Do not redesign the UI.
- Do not refactor project, plot, or GenAI services yet.
- Keep changes small and targeted.
- Preserve existing export smoke tests.

Create if appropriate:
R/services/service_result.R
R/services/export_service.R

If the app is not structured as an R package, place these helpers wherever current app helper functions live, but keep them clearly separated.

Requirements:

1. Add a standard service result helper.

Create a helper like:

service_result <- function(
  status = c("success", "warning", "error", "needs_input"),
  value = NULL,
  artifacts = list(),
  messages = character(),
  warnings = character(),
  errors = character(),
  diagnostics = list(),
  code = NULL,
  metadata = list()
)

It should return a named list.

2. Add export service helpers.

Implement helpers such as:

validate_export_config(export_dir, export_name)

export_html_service(report, export_dir, export_name)

export_code_service(code, export_dir, export_name)

export_all_service(report, code, export_dir, export_name)

3. Validation should handle:
- missing report
- missing code
- missing export_dir
- missing export_name
- invalid filename characters if relevant
- directory does not exist
- directory creation failure
- write permission failure
- AutoPlots::save_widget failure
- writeLines failure

4. Export services should never throw raw errors to Shiny.

Use tryCatch and return service_result(status = "error", errors = ...).

5. On success, return artifacts with paths.

Example:

artifacts = list(
  html_path = "C:/.../report.html",
  code_path = "C:/.../report.R"
)

6. Update Shiny observers for:
- Export HTML
- Export R Code
- Export All

They should:
- call the service
- display messages/errors from the returned service_result
- avoid duplicating validation logic in observers

7. Preserve generated report code behavior.

Do not change the generated code output except where currently required for export paths.

8. Add simple smoke/test helper if practical.

Example:

.ap_qa_export_service <- function()

or a documented manual test section.

9. Smoke tests:
- Export HTML still works.
- Export R Code still works.
- Export All still works.
- Missing report shows friendly error.
- Invalid export directory shows friendly error.
- Existing successful file paths are reported correctly.

build_export_code <- function(export_dir = "path/to/output", export_name = "autoplots_report") {
  paste0(
    "AutoPlots::save_widget(\n",
    "  widget = report,\n",
    "  path = ", r_string(export_dir), ",\n",
    "  name = ", r_string(export_name), ",\n",
    "  selfcontained = TRUE,\n",
    "  overwrite = TRUE,\n",
    "  open = FALSE\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(
  saved_code,
  section_plot_names = list(),
  layout_type = "Grid",
  cols = 2,
  export_dir = "path/to/output",
  export_name = "autoplots_report",
  data_path = "path/to/data.csv"
) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,
    layout_type = layout_type,
    cols = cols
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(", r_string(data_path), ")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    layout_code,
    "\n\n",
    build_export_code(export_dir = export_dir, export_name = export_name)
  )
}

write_report_code <- function(code, path, name) {
  export_dir <- selected_value(path)
  if (is.null(export_dir)) {
    stop("Export directory is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  export_name <- selected_value(name)
  if (is.null(export_name)) {
    stop("File name is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(export_dir)) {
    dir.create(export_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(export_dir)) {
    stop("Export directory could not be created.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  output_path <- file.path(
    normalizePath(export_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE),
    paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(export_name)), ".R")
  )

  writeLines(code, con = output_path, useBytes = TRUE)
  normalizePath(output_path, winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)
}

normalize_project_path <- function(path) {
  path <- selected_value(path)
  if (is.null(path)) {
    stop("Project file path is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!grepl("\\.rds$", path, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    path <- paste0(path, ".rds")
  }

  path
}

Refactored export logic into the service-result pattern.

Changes:
- Added [R/services/service_result.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/services/service_result.R)
  - `service_result()` with the standard fields from the contract.
- Added [R/services/export_service.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/services/export_service.R)
  - `validate_export_config()`
  - `export_html_service()`
  - `export_code_service()`
  - `export_all_service()`
  - `.ap_qa_export_service()`
- Updated [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R)
  - sources the new service files
  - adds `service_result_message()`
  - `Export HTML`, `Export R Code`, and `Export All` observers now call services and display returned messages/errors
  - removed duplicated observer-level export validation logic

Verified:
- Export service smoke passed:
  - R code export writes `.R`
  - missing report returns friendly `needs_input`
  - missing code returns friendly `needs_input`
  - invalid filename returns friendly validation message
  - `AutoPlots::save_widget()` failure is caught as a service error
- Shiny observer smoke passed:
  - Export R Code writes file and displays success
  - no ready report code shows friendly error
  - missing report shows friendly error

Note: HTML success still depends on the installed AutoPlots exposing `save_widget()`, which this R session currently does not.


## 2026-07-04T15:50:36.273Z - user

Topics: autoplots


Extract the Analytics Shiny App from the AutoPlots repo into a separate app repo.

Goal:
Separate the app/product code from the AutoPlots plotting package.

Rules:
- Do not rewrite the app.
- Do not modify AutoPlots plotting functions.
- Do not move AutoPlots internals into the app repo.
- Copy first; do not delete from AutoPlots until the app runs from the new repo.
- The new app repo should depend on AutoPlots as an external package.
- Keep changes small and reversible.

New repo:
<NEW_REPO_NAME>

Migration plan:

1. Identify app-specific files in the AutoPlots repo:
- app.R or main Shiny entry point
- app-specific R helpers
- registries
- services
- project/export helpers
- www assets
- docs/product_scope.md
- docs/service_contract.md
- docs/electron_smoke_test.md
- docs/product_roadmap.md
- sample data if present

2. Copy app-specific files into the new repo.

Suggested structure:
- app.R
- R/services/
- R/registries/
- R/project/
- R/utils/
- www/
- docs/
- inst/sample_data/
- README.md

3. Add a DESCRIPTION or renv/setup file for app dependencies.

Include AutoPlots as an external dependency.

4. Remove any source() calls or relative references that assume the app lives inside AutoPlots.

The app should load AutoPlots with:

library(AutoPlots)

5. Update paths.

Ensure project save/load, bundles, exports, and sample data use paths relative to the app repo or user-selected paths, not the old AutoPlots repo.

6. Add README.md.

Include:
- what the app is
- dependency on AutoPlots
- how to run locally
- how to run Electron smoke test if applicable
- link/reference to Electron wrapper repo
- basic smoke test checklist

7. Run smoke tests:
- app launches from new repo
- upload CSV
- build Line
- add p1
- build Bar
- add p2
- grid preview
- section preview
- export HTML
- export R code
- save/load project
- save/load bundle

8. After the new repo passes, leave a note in AutoPlots docs pointing to the app repo.

Do not delete the old app copy from AutoPlots until explicitly requested.

## 2026-07-04T15:55:55.521Z - user

## Session Meta
- id: `019f28e3-50a4-7141-bd00-6267c32b0abe`

## 2026-07-04T15:56:08.477Z - assistant

Yes, your memory is right for an R package: files in nested folders under `R/` are not treated like normal package R source files during build/load. Package R code should live directly in `R/*.R`.

For this app repo, `R/services/` works only because `app.R` explicitly does:

```r
source(file.path("R", "services", "service_result.R"))
source(file.path("R", "services", "export_service.R"))
```

So it is fine for a plain Shiny app, but if we want this repo to behave cleanly as an R package or package-like app, we should flatten those files.

Recommended adjustment:

```text
R/
  service_result.R
  export_service.R
```

Then update `app.R` to:

```r
source(file.path("R", "service_result.R"))
source(file.path("R", "export_service.R"))
```

That keeps the service boundary concept without fighting R package conventions. I’d make that small cleanup next.


## 2026-07-04T15:56:27.641Z - user

Yes, make that cleanup

## 2026-07-04T15:59:26.306Z - user

Important R package structure correction:

Do not create subdirectories inside R/.

This app repo may be structured as an R package or package-like project, so all R source files should live directly under R/.

Use filename prefixes instead of nested folders.

Preferred structure:

R/
  app_server.R
  app_ui.R
  service_result.R
  service_export.R
  service_project.R
  service_plot.R
  registry_plots.R
  registry_options.R
  project_state.R
  project_bundle.R
  utils_paths.R
  utils_messages.R

Do not use:
R/services/
R/registries/
R/project/
R/utils/

If any code or docs still reference nested R folders, update them to the flat R/ file structure.
